France is a beautiful contry, visited by millions of people each year. But if you are invited to a French person's home, do you know the correct etiquette? Here are solme tips: 1. DON'T be late. Make sure you arrive a few minutes early and wait. 2. DON'T sit too close to people. Some people stand so close that I can smell what they had for breakfast. You're expected to leave at least two feet between you and the person you're speaking with. 3. DON'T cross your arms across your chest, unless you're a king or queen. People might think you're angry or bored.  4. DON'T ask personal questions. If you ask questions that are considered rude  'How much money do you make?' 'How old are you?' 'Are you married?'  you might not get an honest answer.

In my office, I was always working very hard and I didn't have any promotion! One day, I needed some advice of other colleague. Her desk was near the director's desk and she was very appreciated by him. She told me I would meet the director to tell him my request. So, I did! I was very satisfied because he received me very well and listened to me with attention. So, he gave me a positive answer. I was very happy and I thanked him for his great help. In the same time, I decided to show better all that I was doing in my work and explain with more and more details to value my goals. It was successful! What wasn't my surprise when he promoted me!

I'm a young entrepreneur. I'm just 27 years old and I've just finished my studies in Management. I love read books, newspapers. Before, I bought so much books in libraries when I wanted to read one. I went to the library with my car and bought the book I needed. And after came back home. It took me a lot of time to get a book or a journal I needed to read. But since I've bought a tablet, I feel like all is now easier. For be informed about the economic situation, the companies situations, or the political situation where I project to invest around the world, I need to get some informations as quickly as possible. Before when I went to the kiosk to get a newspaper or a business magazine, that took me a lot of time. And now with my tablet, I just have to click on an icone in the screen of my tablet and, without taking time to go to the kiosk, I have my journal or my mazagine in my tablet and I can read it immediatly. What took me ten minutes before, takes me now just hum... one minute.%% I enjoy how the media with technology get us the life easier than before.

Antismoking efforts are hard to avoid nowadays in France. Since 5 years, it's not allowed to smoke in a public place. Smoking in a bar, a restaurant is prohibited. If the regulations were violated, smokers faced a fine of 68 euros. From a political point of view, this law had good intentions: to protect all the citizen from an dangerous atmosphere. We breathe better. Non-smokers are happy: they win:"it stinks of tobacco in here" they used to say. It's finished, pubs smell good, an so healthy air that a baby could have his night there. Even when it's raining, smokers goes out, in front of the bar or restaurant to continue to puff on their cigarettes. During the evening, every time we want to smoke, we quit the table, we get dressed again. New laws, new habits. A kind of smoker's brotherhood exists on sidewalks in the night: conviviality is not inside but outside! If you ask me, I love to light up when I have a drink in hand and it's more complicated, less comfortable now. In fact, going out "somewhere" it's to stay up in the street and the cold.

Actually I consider that my life is pretty good: I have a roof over my head, food on the table. I can watch, hear, walk along the street in a free country. However I have a job that makes sense, life can be hard and stressful. Something is definitely missing in our western society: people always complain about their life. More we have everything we need, more our dissatisfaction rise up. The high quality of our life -with all modern conveniences- doesn't seem to be enough to smile more! What a paradox! Perhaps I'm wrong but I just search now to simplify my way of life...I don't dream about more money, I appreciate a hearty meal shared with friends or family more than buy a new car.Feeling of happiness isn't measured by material possessions. My social life isn't hectic and demanding,I hate every kind of obligations. In business it's important to network whenever possible. Fortunately, my lifestyle is far from those problems. What I need is absolutly less stress. And more time. I like the idea of "degrowth", this movement that advocate "voluntary simplicity ".

text: My Smartphone is my best friend; It and me are inseparable. At 7 o'clock, he said me "wake up". My first reaction is to throw it in the bedroom. But finally I take it and talk to him ! "what about the weather ?" "What are my appointements today ?". Since I bought it, I forgot all phone numbers, all addresses, the subway plan. Since I bought it, I have no pen, no paper in my bag. I need to purchase something ? I note it on my smartphone ! It is my memory ! Until the black day, when a black screen don't awaked me in the morning, when the same black screen has forgot my doctor's appointement and don't say me to buy milk. It the same day that I realized that I need seriously to begin a cure detox. Then my friend and me are separate all day from 9 pm. Until 7 am of course.

Is gender equality still questionable in 2013 in France? According to several researches, women hold more leadership position than in the past but still earn less than men for equal responsibility. Women tends to demonstrate higher skills however they continue being perceived as weaker than men. The maternity leave is generally seen as an opportunity for men to steel a job position without feeling remorse. Even though women have the right now to stand for better job, they are still required to fulfill their housewife duties without ever complaining about the burden of their complex daily life.

text: In my studies I used to make many presentations in english and spanish. I remember a presentation in english that I made with a colleague, this presentation was about rugby and british identity. I was in charge of the describing part of the differents rugby teams in Great Britain. I explained the history of each team, the symbols and the anthems. My colleague made the part that describes how rugby can reflect the british identity. Her part was more general than my mine. At the end we concluded that as French people we could believe that rugby has an important place in Great Britain but in reality football is most famous, in fact like in France. Roles do males and females have changed a lot since fifty years. 50 years ago, women stayed at home, they didn't work outside. Women had to cook and take care about their family. Whereas men won money to provide the family's need. Gradually things have changed, women have begun work outside. What women can do in business ? Nothing ? This had to be difficult for women to integrate themselves into the working world. Now, this is maybe still difficult. Women are still less paid than men, there are still differences between men and women. Now days men still like win money for the family and be the "boss" at home. But this isn't like that, currently men take care of their children, some men are househusbands.

One day, when I was in high school, we were having a math class in a classroom which was locked. So, we had to wait because the teacher was always coming late. My friend got an idea and persuaded me to hide inside a locker, and when the second friend who was late would come, he would give me a signal by coughing, so I would jump out of the locker to scare him. Well, my friend was actually an idiot because he told the other friend the plan, and coughed when our science teacher was walking around my hiding place. So, I jumped out of the locker doing "waaah" and nearly crashed into her. She was almost sixty, small and thin as a rake. She didn't even blink, but just gave me a quick cold look, and asked if I was all right with a face saying something like does this often happen to me. I just mumbled that I was sorry and she left.

We use to spend a lot of time with other people. Those people can be our family, our colleagues and every person we meet outside at all. What's most important for me is to consider the other like a real person. That is to say, you have to smile, to be polite, to welcome the people you meet. Our society doesn't make ourselves be generous with others : life is hectic, for a lot of people making money is the main activity in life, people have difficulties to dicuss without shouting in general. Every day, I try to do a good action, that's my involvement to change that situation. People must accept the other. People must discover the other. People must consider the other. Like it was said during that lesson, life is short and we can't waste time. Be happy and feel free depend on how we consider the others.

I advise you to observe all the other invitees and to copy their behaviour, I think that it the best way to learn and to avoid mistakes. First, turn your mobile off, it's very unpleasant to hear a ringing at the table and to interrupt the discussion. When you pour wine, don't fill fully your glass, you have to taste it first. Don't speak with food in the mouth, don't put your elbows on the table, don't drink too fast... You may bring for instance a bottle of wine, your host could sometimes open it during the meal to share and taste it, creating a conviviality moment and a conversation topic. Avoid not to let anyone else speak, but on an other side, take a part in the discussion, otherwise, people will believe that you aren't interested in the conversation topic. Don't forget to congratulate your host about the quality of the meal, it's always pleasant !

Many differences stay between male and female genders in my country. Many women work but they do also much more housework than men ! They always do ironing, shopping, laundry and other chores. They also have to educate children, particularly when they are babies or young children. But who knows ? Maybe someday men and women will have the same role in their house ? In fact, things evolve slowly. More and more men like to educate and look after their children and more an more men do house chores. But there is one place where the equality doesn't evolve enough, it is the world of work. Women earn 20% less than men and they often can't have jobs as important as men. However several laws to reduce this inequality have been voted. Why does nothing move in spite of laws ?

In my country, these days, men and women are equal. The women's movement of the 1970s has stopped definitively the "old" roles males and females. If I think it was necessary to fight for women's rights, time has really changed. It's not a shame to say, as a woman, I "just" want take care of my family. The young generation is more flexible than my parents. For example, it's not unusual to see a father staying at home when they have a baby, because his wife have a job with more responsabilities. The male or female identity isn't blocked in a only way for all the life.That's very important for both genders... Men don't consider unthinkable to cook, to do the dishes or bring the children to the school. A woman today can have a great career and a family life. In France the situation is enough comfortable for women.

I have the chance to live in Paris and to have access to a very large range of cultural events. In my neighborhood, I have a famous concert hall that I discovered quite recently although I am living here since 10 years. I have very eclectic tastes in terms of music like pop-rock, metal, RnB or classic. The first time I went to this concert hall was quite by chance, I had a ticket given by a friend. What I felt during this classic concert was absolutely unbelievable! First, you have the ambiance of a large and impressive place, then the imposing presence of the orchestra and finally the power of the music that brings you in a disconnected world for 2 hours. From the first notes I had goosebumps, I was literally fascinated, time was suspended to the piano chords. I can't wait to go to the Beethoven concert tomorrow, even if I am currently listening Metallica!

In my country (Morocco), there is a law that imply that arab couples cannot be able to make a reservation in a hotel without the act of wedding. On the other hand, all western tourists can do it, and no matter if this tourist is with her girlfriend or a prostitute. One day, I was with my girlfriend on vacation and we were looking for a place where to sleep, it was very difficult because we are arabs and muslims. When you ask the hotel manager, he explain that the law is that way because of religious aspect : In Islam it's a sin to have a girlfriend and to sleep with her before getting married, so that's why we are not allowed to get a hotelroom reservation. Also, he said that we apply the law in each case, we are muslim, so we must respect the law of arab countries, and if you are european for example, then we will apply laws of your country and this in Morocco. They believe that if they apply islamic laws to the tourists, the economy will decrease swiftly. I can understand this point of view, wich is purely economic, but I think that we are a little bit hypocrite in Morocco, we have to whether seperate religion from the state or totally apply the islamic laws for every one.%%

Dear Mr Malcolm McDonald,  I am writing with regard to the breach of my contract by TransWorld Enterprises.  As you know, I worked during the two last weekends and the company has refused to pay for this overtime.  After having given it much thought and as for my lawyer's advice, I am considering pursuing this matter to the courts. Furthermore, I am planning to take the moral high ground.  As a result of the company objection to pay my due, I am asking for a financial compensation.  I reaffirm here my will to reach an agreement out of court. If the firm keeps not taking my claims into account, therefore I will be within my rights to institue legal proceedings against TransWorld Enterprises.  I look forward to hearing from you.  Yours sincerely.

I've been working for 20 years. Therefore I can say that I'm now  able to easily identify office politics. Initially I was the one to pretend that office politics are not a game to be played. I was rather considering that our abilities are essential to get promoted. Now my opinion has been changed. I definitely consider that life at work is a game that everyone should play. Otherwise you will never be integrated into your company. Basicaly everyone should consider that there is time every day to talk with colleagues in order to improve our relationship. By doing this, we will be more appreciated by our colleagues who can advocate us once it's necessary. But for me it's an effort as it's not part of my personality. Like many people I tend to only talk to  persons I like working with. But it's a fact that everyone needs to be a " team player ". It's more an more one of the first crieria we take into account when we assess people ( yearly people review). I'd like to focus on one important point as well. The main external threaths are competition and economical environment, the main internal threaths are : the lack of trust and motivation from the employees ; a bad organisation and communication within the company. The internal threaths can be real weaknesses for the company and affect the expansion, competitivity and profits. Nowadays some solutions are put in place to resolve this kind of weaknesses. Brainstorming and team building are the most current methods used to improve communication and trust inside of the company. Also it's really important for the managers to encourage the motivation and commitment of the employees. Involving people into a project it's a critical success factor and requires to lead interesting meetings, to discuss and listen ideas.  In conclusion any company must consider their employees like a first asset, must promote a corporate culture. The key of the success is the communication and consideration between individuals.

As for me and my husband, both retired, we must consider us as very lucky people. I am worrying about my son. He decided to buy a flat, which was a rather good idea at a moment when the housing market dropped off. The problem is that when you buy something you have to sell an other one, and the dropping out is the same in both situations. So, we had to help him. It was a pleasure to lend him money even if I am not sure at all that he will be able to pay us back. An other issue is that his 'partner' (in fact the mother of their little child) earns much more money than him. Being a civil servant, she has an employment security which is not his.

We are going to adopt in my country a very strange law relating to the right to abort. This right was granted to women in 1976 provided their pregnancy was less than 10 weeks . Nothing else was required and in particular the position that the father may have has not been taken into consideration. It is a right that women have Some associations since 1976 have raised relevant issues following the implementation of this law . Among others the fact that the number of abortions was supposed to decrease while it remains stable at 200 000 per year or the need to properly inform those who have decided to abort about all the aspects of an abortion. Now our new government wants to adopt a law that will forbid any kind of information which could be considered as 'against abortion' , typically some websites , family advisors or even priests . Apparently authorities are fearing a new position of a significant part of the population who consider that we have gone to far as basically we can do almost anything that we want with the 1976 law and its subsequent amendments. It looks pretty strange to face such a situation in a country that claims that it is the country of the human rights and freedom of expression !%%!

Are the roles of males and females the same nowadays? Even if there have been a lot of evolution since the early 1900's, I think that there is still inequality between women and men in France.  Regarding workplace, do you really think men and women are equal? Well you wish. Men still in majority have better job with more responsibility and earn more money than women. However, this tend to change with time. We observe more and more big companies' CEO as women. Yet in 1900's, women didn't go to school or didn't have a degree as high as men because they used to have children earlier. In 2013, women are more focused in their career so they spend more time at school and have kids later.  At home, the roles have changed. More women work full time as their husband. Do you remember how it was before WWII? Women stayed at home taking care of their children.  Do you think women are happy with this evolution. Probably yes, but it needs to be improved still.

text: When I was young I loved horses, and I took the first opportunity for riding one. As a result of my passion, my father bought me a horse and I could move around our house in our beautiful the country when and where I wanted ! After many years, when I earned money enough, I went to stables for taking riding horse lessons with an instructor who was a former famous jumper. He allowed me to have a good level in show jumping. But I had also many possibilities to attend serious accidents, especially the death of a horse in a show jumping competition. This accident made me consider that this sport was dangerous also for horses. Afterwards I was afraid for my horse and gradually this anxiety hindered me to riding properly. At the end I stopped competition and show jumping. Now I do only dressage.

I really wanted to study aboard, prior to that I wanted to study where I live, in France then get a licence in LEA which is about English and Spanish and afterwards I would work as a movie translator in France ir someplace else.

Since i was born i feel like things are getting worse and worse. The famous crisis is getting permanent. It seems to me that i've always known that global crisis. Unfortunately we are not concerned the same way. I can afford a car, a place to live and food for my whole family other do not have that chance. That world has that drawback, though it can be wonderful, nothing is fair, nothing is shared. Everyone could be happy and live well. But we all expect the best, without caring the price of that terrible wish... Let us be optimistic, and how about living in a better world, where money would not be a problem, for anyone !!

In France, since two years the bottom is dropping out of the national economy. A lot of people are loosing their jobs, taxes and prices are increasing too much. We can see that more more people are pessimistic and sometimes even depressive.  The national debt is increasing every year, our country is becoming impoverished and no serious plans are developped. The media are also responsible for this bad state of mind, they are always speaking about all that and I am sure that they could, from time to time, speak about positive things. That would help to keep a dynamic and positive attitude. So when you have a roof over your head and food on the table you can consider that all is fine for you. You can learn to be happy and enjoy being without too many problems.

If media has changed in my lifetime ? Yes totaly! When I was growing up, there was no TV, no  laptops , no tablet and no cellphone. In the evening, people heard musique , read books, or brought them together for telling about the  daly event. Now a day, there are many kinds of media in the modern world. Communications media had totaly change the life. We can access anytime,  anywhere ! Actualy I write this texte with my tablet and I can swipe the screen to turn pages! Amazing ! Just a few years ago , nobody could belive that happen now ! A friend of mine said me I want to sell his house  because he  don't receive the internet to much well ! With my tablet or my smartphone I can call, take picture, hear musique, send messages, and stay current on the news one line.  But in the other way, I don't like read with my tablet. It's a pleasure  for me to go  bed with a paper book. I like curting up in my bed and read novels. Reading is my escape and I read until I 'll fall asleep .

Are there still many gender differences in France ? I think there will always be. After the world war II, mentalities have changed. In 1944, women can vote as men, they become more emancipated. Today, women can be independant, earn money and work like men. However, the equality is not for tomorrow. There are still many traditions in France. The "traditionnal" woman's place is at home. But this housewife's role doesn't inhibit a woman to work. Unfortunately, in 2013 men still ear 16% more than women ! Nowaday, men are more thoughtful with women : they help for cooking, chores... Nevertheless, the division is not equal and many women have a "double day" when they go back home. But some question are more complex: the custody of the children in case of divorce, for instance, is generally accord to the women. Are the men unable to take care of children ? I don't think so. So, is the fully emancipation of women possible ? There will always be a difference between men and women, in one way it can be unfair, and in other way it is just justified.

When I was growing up, when I wanted to read a novel I must buy a book. If I wanted to be on top of information about word life I must buy a newspaper; when I wanted to learn geography or history at school, I must buy notebooks. Nowadays, only forty years later everything has changed: you don't need anymore papers. With only one tablet, you can do what you want. You can download all books you'd like to read; you can directly purchase clothes; you can book a table in a restaurant; you can look for a new job; you can listen to music; you can order a taxi. In a while you can do everything that you need in your every day life. Yes! it is very simple for everyone but for me, never beats a good book, I mean, I need this contact with the paper, it's sensual. Never beats a good concert, with all the instruments and players If you love music like me. So I believe that progress is good for the ability to accede quickly to everything but I need to keep few of uses. Broadband definitely is a fantastic way of exchange.%% You can communicate everywhere with everyone in the world in a split second.

I remember that a friend of mine, who lives abroad, came to France for business. He asked me how he had to behave at the table with his French partners. Here was my advice : In France, its essential to respect good manners when someone invites you at home or to a restaurant. Its important to follow these rules : 1) Before meal: You have to wait to sit down until your host invites you to do so and indicates your place. After sitting down, place your napkin on your lap. Put the two hands on the table, on each side of the cutlery. Its impolite to put your elbows on the table. 2) During the meal: Do not take bread before the 1st dish has been served. You're supposed to wait for your host to begin eating. Use the cutlery the most distant from the plate first to finish with the closest ones. It's shocking to talk with your mouth full. Do not reach out to take something that is far from you, but to ask politely your neighbours of table. It's rather impolite to use your phone at the table. 3) After meal: Do not put down your cutlery on the table, but on the plate in a parallel way.%% It's polite to place the napkin not folded on table when you finish eating. You must wait until your host invites you to leave the table.

Do gender differences remain in my country ? May I question myself about it ? I don't think so. Even if things have improved those past years, some differences between men and women remain the same. The most part of the population is ready to reach for equality but no one agree about the way to reach it. While some want to split the bill, others consider that men have to pay it. Within a family, everyone is capable of cleaning the house. So why it is mainly done by women ? What to think about salaries ? The pattern looks the same, but things have definitely changed. First of all, women doesn't have to ask permission to live their life, second of all, many acts are result of decisions between men and women, but a lot remains to be done mainly through companies.

The main purpose of this excerpt is to provide some information about how people should react when they face a career path confusion. The author uses an interesting way to explain that workers must focus on their feelings, hobbies or needs to determine which job could make them happier, which job could correspond the most with their personality. Even though it is likely not so difficult to find such a position, I guess the author forgot many important points, especially in the current crisis which has been affecting all the industries. Obviously, people must choose a position which will satisfy their needs, but according to their skills and experiences, not only feelings. When you apply for a position, the motivation is absolutely not enough for your employer, you have to have at your disposal a real background, the requested diplomas and sometimes must be strongly recommended by another people.

topic: Helping a coworker deal with a phobia
text: Technology has completely changed my professional life, not so much my private life. Let me elaborate a little bit what I want to say. When I joined my company, thirty years ago, email or cell phone did not exist yet and we spent hours on the phone spending our time at our desk . Today , thanks to emails or cell phones, you can be reached almost wherever you are . However this is a double edged sword as there is a tendency to consider that you are supposed to be very reactive , much more than in the past because you have got new tools. An other trend is the over use of audio conferences involving people who are using their cell phones (because they are travelling or for any other reason). To me this should be an exception as it is poor communication. In my private life, I do not think technology has really changed anything.

text: The last sales meeting where I attempted , was really clear about the target which must be achieved by the sales department next year. I heard with pleasure the presentation of our manager about the difficulties meet over this year and the solutions taken to keep the customers. Also the presence of the CEO to this sales meeting showed us that the sales department had a real strategic mission in our company and we had to carry on our effort. However I have some remarks about this sales meeting . Firstly if our manager had announced the key points at the opening of the meeting it would have been easier for us to be more attentive; secondly if each sales representative had presented their sales action over the year it would have been more constructive. Thirdly if our manager had let the sales representatives talk it would have allowed to the CEO to show their commitment. Except the below bad points , this sales meeting remain positive and  it will be usefull for the next year.

In France, we have a lot of musical comedies and plays in each city. Musical comedies take place all over the country, as a tour in the biggest towns. My family loves to see these shows. We love the way the production is implemented, and all of the details they had to think of. The costumes are absolutely exquisites, the dancing and the play of the characters are marvellous as well. We love to take part of the adventure they share with this kind of show. On the other hand, most of the plays talk about society aspects or social behaviours, in a humoristic context. If you want to see a play, you have to be prepared to laugh during all the show. It's amazing how the characters make fun about a society or social subject just by their play. I think all these shows are some interesting activities to do to have fun and share good moments with friends, and family. We used to first think of the cinema or the bowling, but we don't have to forget the others events our city can bring us.

When I was growing up, in the middle 1960s, internet didn't be found, regardless general public didn't use it in France. The only media we knew were books, newspapers and television; however, they were less widespread than today. It's difficult to imagine, but everybody didn't own a television. Subsequently, we really were less informed, and especially less quickly, about what happened in the world. Dictator's life was far easier... Internet was a groundbreaking invention, which changed the world as deeply as the industrial revolution did in the last 19th century. As for me, internet totally changed my way of learning. Nowadays, if I search for an information, at any time, about any topic, I take my laptop and I immediately access to any article, anywhere in the world. It's absolutely fantastic! In the past, I must go to the library and, with luck, I could consult a few books and nothing else. When I was a schoolboy, I learned English with a book; today, I curl up on my couch and I connect to Englistown. I study by watching video clips, and my private teacher lives in Capetown! The only thing that internet didn't change for me is my way of reading books: I continue to prefer paper.%%

The last couple of years was difficult for the country where I live. Because of the political turmoil and war the economy was down. Many people lost their job or had financial problems because they had to take charge of their extended family ,who was affected by the war. The market price was high and people struggle to have food on their table. Fortunately things are looking up now. There are more and more people who can pick up works. It still difficult for people to be thriftier because of the rising of market place. The government is managing to keep the inflation rate low. I think in the future things would turning around.

In France, when you are arriving into the restaurant you are expected to go first before women if you are a man, because all the people inside will like at you and not the woman. So, at the table, you have to let seat the woman first. It's bad manners to put elbows or hats on the table. Manners have to do with showing respect toward others.You are supposed to wait every seat before eating.What is proper is to not talk with a mouth full.You are expected to stand up if a woman was to get up to go to the restroom. After sitting down to eat, place your napkin on your lap.Remember,never use you cell phone at the dinner table.

I like my life. Philosophically, I think I am always at the place I must be at a given time : because what I am, what I do is linked directly with my previous thoughts, behaviors, and actions. Of course, like every human being, I often dream about having a better position, a bigger income so I could purchase a bigger house and car. However, in twenty years in the same company, I had five different positions and each one was better than the one before. I don't see why this evolution should be different in the future: I always try to do my best. Nevertheless, I have two challenges: to master my level of stress in keeping a very regular practice of sports and to have a more satisfacting social life, I mean, to bind ou rebind deeper links with my friends and maybe to found a family.

When you receive an invitation for a party, you must reply relatively quickly because your host can think you're waiting for a better invitation. At the party, it's thoughtful to bring a small gift , bottle of wine or flowers  to your hostess. You must be polite with the other friends and introduce yourself each other. When person are sit down,  if a woman or an elderly person come,  all the gentlemen would stand up. Don't take or receive phonecall, or send text messages with your smartphone . At dinner table, eat carefuly, slowly and  use cultery correctly .  Don't slurp ! Don't yell  or don't talk about politic, religion  or money. It"s impolite to get up for going to the restroom when you are on the table. Put your napkin on your lap and don't put your elbows on the table. When you live the party,  said how happy you are and few days after send a short thank you note or email to your host.

text: I am afraid that I am totally dependent on my smartphone! Prior to that, I was using a cell phone and didn't want to buy a smartphone because I thought  that it was not a basic need. It's not really a funny game for me to participate to the office politics. However, even in a small company like the one I'm working for, it's absolutely impossible to avoid it. During years my boss used to be like a mentor for me and I was following most of his advices and agreeing to many of his points of view. For me it was not a strategy but a kind of respect toward him. With time, the more I increased in the company, the more I understood that behind the smiles we can find a large deal of hypocrisy. My mentor was able to use my skills and my support against some other colleagues, not to the benefit of the company, but just to get a higher position in it and whenever it was possible to the detriment of those he was considering as enemies. Now, I know how to share things and I prefer to follow the one who seems to be right rather than the one who can be considered as an ally.

Given that, I am still a student I will discuss about office politics in school. To my mind, office politics are not really used in University, we do not really see students using different tactics to be successful. Everything is based on talent and the amount of work you intent to dedicate in order to be at the top of your class. By contrast, I think in high school students were using more spin tactics in the aim to be popular or be elected as a class representative. I guess some people just master these tactics since a long time, in order to obtain what they desire. As regards my thinking of office politics, I think that to employ successful strategies you need to be very smart and not show your game like in a poker game. The bedrock of office politics lie on the ability to master the environment and the people surrounding yourself without appearing too ambitious and selfish.

I am pricing coordinator within a forwarder, my job requests somes qualities : reactivity , accurate, commitment . Sometimes , it can be stressful . Also I get married and mother of one child. text: section A: employee basic package - positive feedback on pay scale, vacation days and sick days. - concerns on the overtime culture within the company especially on how they are rewarded.  - remediation actions: managers have to set the exemple, improve the rewarding of overtime.  Section B: employee advanced package - satisfaction on all the areas. - complaints on the required year of working within the company to be entitled to get the advanced package. - shorten the period to eighteen month.  Section C: working environment - overall positive opinion on the equipment provided and clear understanding of the aims and objectives. - issue raised on the lack of feedback. - enhance the formalization of the appraisal system.  Section D: company structure and management - huge concerns on the lines of reporting which are considered too long - increase the responsability of lower manager to fasten the decision-making process. - flatten out the company structure.

Dear Sir, I am writing regarding the overtime I am due . I had to work extra hours during two weekends and I object that you refuse to pay this overtime. My contract clearly states that I should be compensated for my weekend work. Furthermore, you argue that these extra hours weren't your fault, this argument can't be considered as nothing in my contract allows you to refuse to pay me for something the company is not responsible of.  Consequently I believe I am within my rights to claim being paid for the two weekends I had to work. Should you refuse to pay, I would consider the possibility of suing the company for damages. I hope there is an opportunity to settle out of court. Yours sincerely MAP

In France we like to say that our country is the (father) land of human right. But we don't use the word "human" for this. In french, we use the word "men", which could be translated in "human" or in "male". So literally human right are men rigth. But does it means something in the french way of life? Fortunately, this is not the same case in french society. Even woman had obtained the right to vote only 50 years ago, they can today access easier to responsibility at work and men are more prsent at home. But there's a lot of steps for a real equality. Politician, CEO of big company are still kind of position detained by male.

text: Since I whas child I love technology. When I whas 6, my parents bought a new television with the color and I remend how it's hard for them to found the channel. For me it was totally natural, and I used it naturally. After that my parent bought one one the first PC, an Amstrad. I was captivated and used it every day. My interest for technology never stop, and actually I have computers, tablets, smartphone and lots of gadgets. My friend always call me for informations about new product or for help them with computer or smartphone. But it's sometimes a problem because I always want to buy new product. I'm totally sad wen I can't have the last update or the new generation  smartphone or tablet. Unfortunately I nedd lot of time to keep informed and the downside is the frustration to keep an old product and want to change every day. It's the bad way to be a nerd.

Of course there is still a big gender inequality in my country (France). Even if it's far from being the worst there is still a big effort to do. My country is really attached to tradition value and we are kind of conservative. Thanks god, it's changing but, as far as i'm concern, not fast enough. In the other hand, i might be too much in hurry, if we consider the presence of women in education. Indeed, we may say that women and men are equal in education just for 10-20 years. In my Country (France) people who are smoking aren't allowed to smoke in night clubs , bar and restaurant. Nowadays, It's against the law to smoke in those publics places. It was difficult for a lot of persons to be agree whith that law because they was too addicted about cigarettes. I think it 's still tought for them to follow that law. From my point of view , I agree about it! Because I don't smoke and hate respirate that smog when I eat or I dancing on the floor. I'm a really healthy person and I care about my healt thus it's important for me that this law was applicated. But in other hand , I can understand for smokers it is not easy for them to stop smoking directly when they come in a restaurant and just eat without their cigarette.

When I was young, we didn't have a TV at home and we used a radio to stay informed of what happened in the world. I remember that it enabled to listen to news by accomplishing daily tasks. After, we bought a TV, which became our biggest source of information. We ate in front of the television news, and we enjoyed the possibility to watch images illustrating the news enabling us to understand them better. The newspapers offered the same qualities, but it represented a cost to buy them daily and we couldn't do anything else by reading them. Nowadays, I use the web to get informed. As a matter of fact, I think that it brings almost all advantages : we have a infinite and free source of information, we can look at different sites to examine all kinds of thinkings enabling us to create one's own opinion. We can even sometimes let a comment or read the remarks of other readers, creating sometimes interesting debates and identify better what is at stake.

text: Hi, Ludo You was absent last week, so I send you a summary of the meeting. The CEO explained us the company had to create a new website, in order to improve their benefits. A new website is very expensive, around 20000$. Despite the price, the CFO agreed and informed us we were quite financially sound. However, the COO disagreed with them. According to him, we were in an simple and easy site, that's why we received a lot of orders. Furthermore, he argued that a new version with a new design could shock clients. Finally the CEO, decided to select a customer sample, who will test a beta version during 3 months, in order to have an opinion. BR, Alain

Smoking can harm the health and the government tries to limit as much as possible its consumption. Every year, new laws are voted to discourage consumers to go on. First, in a financial point of view, taxes on packs of cigarettes are often increased : the rising percentage of the prices is significant, so a lot of smokers complain that smoking is becoming a luxury. Furthermore, one tries to disgust consumer from tobacco : some advertisings make them feel guilty, and the producers are obliged to display on the packs shocking images revealing tobacco disastrous effects on the body. At last, smoking has been forbidden in more and more public places (restaurants, coffees...). Some smokers consider that such legislation represent a blow to individual rights, but the others have the right not to breathe smoke. As a matter of fact, non-smokers must be protected from passive smoking, which can have bad consequences.

When I was a child we had no TV at home. So I was used to read a lot, in fact all the time! Now I'm still a avid reader. Anywhere I go, libraries and bookshops are my favourite places.I like watching books covers, it attracts me irresistibly, I like the smell of the paper. When I travel, I never forget to take a book. I can share it with my friends. I never have technological problems, it works always, I just have to read. I never try to read my novels on a tablet. However I can read news from all the world, or gardening blogs or find incredible recipes on an electronic support. At the moment, I have not an e-reader nor a tablet. So my experiences are limited to read on my computer. What I regret is that takes time: internet is like an infinite galaxy. Often, I have the sensation when I switch off the computer, after two or more hours surfing, reading a lot of informations, that nothing really substantial stays in my brain. Nowadays  new language and new expressions come from the new technologies. My reading habits have changed but not that much!%%

In this day and age, despite what sometimes people use to say (especially elderly people), the etiquette still exists. In France, like in other countries, when you are invited at someone's or somewhere, for dinner, or an other special occasion, there are some rules that you have to respect. First, don't be late, or too late: when you are invited at someone's place, the best is to arrived few minutes after the scheduled hour, but not more than fifteen minutes. If you are going to an event, like, let's say, in an Art Gallery, for some kind of event, just be at time, or five minutes late. Then, an other advice is to bring something, a little present, when you're invited for dinner somewhere (except if you're going to the restaurant). The most common things are a bouquet of flowers, or a bottle of wine, but other things are also appreciated, like some special food, to taste at the aperitif. Of course, in all cases, turn off your phone before going to your invitation, because it is ill-considered, and very rude. Then, when comes the time of start eating, wait for everybody to be served before starting, it's respectful.%% Well, now you're ready to spend a good time at your next invitation. Last step : book a flight to France to taste some nice food !

In my country proper etiquette is going rarer. Maybe isn't it adapted to this day and age ? Anyway, being polite to a dinner still remains very important, especially in France, where we often sit down a very long time to eat. Even if, I guess, to put the napkin on the laps or to use the right cutleries with all kind of forks, is not expected from people anymore, waiting for the hostess seated before starting to eat still matters a lot ! However, I must admit that, most of the time, only basic manners are respected (like don't speaking with mouth full or not receive calls during the meal, that's to say no bad manners), while the utterly etiquette is typically applied for formal parties, by the Elderlies or in traditionnal families. Finally, when we want to thank the hostess, we generally offer a small gift (like a bouquet of flower, chocolates or a bottle of wine) when arriving, and after congratulate her for the reception, so that she's reassured about the success of her dinner !

In 2006, a law was voted by the french government that forbid smoking in public places. If you don't respect this law, we committed a felony, this is a infringement to the law and have to pay a fine of sixty eight euros. Personally, I think this is a good decision of our government. Indeed, this law oblige smokers to respect people around them. Before this law, we could be in a bus and support the awful smells of smoking. It was constraining. Moreover, this law permit a lot of smokers to stop their bad custom by forcing them to go outside for smoke, even for one cigarette. To my mind it's globally a good thing but in the other side, I think that some smokers have a real problem with their abduction, I mean this is like a medical problem, like they're sick by smoking. In general, they're people stressed everytime. I think that government shuld think about a psychological monitoring for treat these people fundamentally.

Until very recently, i was employed by a european agency. I earned about three thousands euros a month with benefits. I am currently living on my leaving package and eventualy on unemployment benefit, if i manage to get it. I don't want to get back to be an employee, so i am considering investing this package and my savings in my own business. I have enough to keep a rough over my head for a few months and I have plenty of food on the table. My first move would be to go to london, but i still need to figure out how to actually set the business.

No-smokers are not longer troubled by the cigaret's odors and now smokers couldn't consider now to smoke in a bar or in their office. In my view, this kind of law is really usefull for our society ,  when this law has been applied I was smoker and I've never considered this law like unacceptable but like a new way of life. From my point of view the persons who haven't yet accepted this law and carry-on to criticize are too addict to the the cigaret. Their drug is most important than their neighbours, childrens or friends' health.

text: I always wanted take up white-water rafting. I love the water sports. When I was young I begun sport with canoeing in calm waters, little by little I wanted more thrills, so I took up white-water kayaking. Next I practiced this sport in gorgeous place, with huge rivers, sometimes even the ocean. I really loved this sport. But one day, I saw people who did white-water rafting. It seemed to be awesome, and also very scared. I didn't understand why, but I felt afraid of this sport whereas I always practiced extremes sports, like hang gliding, snowboarding, windsurfing or also skydiving. One day I decided to face my fears of white-water rafting, so I enjoyed down the river. To finish, I want to tell that I'll never regret to face my fears, and I plan to face others fears. And I recommend to everyone to face your fears.

In my country, France, manners and etiquette can vary quite significantly from a region to an other region . It is as well very much depending on the social class you are refering to.  For example placing carefully your guests around the dining table is a critical exercise for a typical dinner in the upper class while it has less significance in other contexts . We have very precise rules if needed to place a bishop or a MP etc....These rules are useful to avoid any mistake which can hurt some people... and damage your reputation ! After all,  knowing these rules allows to concentrate on the most important thing which is the quality of the conversation associated to the quality of the food (not to mention wines which could be a subject by itself !). To achieve this you have to make your guests feeling comfortable But there is one sentence which is definitely prohibited and which I have never heard in France and it is to start the meal by saying 'bon appetit' !

Equality between men and women? Not yet in African countries. At home, at work, in the society there are still differences between both genders. At home, women who dont work are still responsible for domestic work, whereas men,even if they dont work, dont do those works. Even when women work they do some of the housework. Men, as before, provide the subsistence for the family and are chiefs of families. This structure is usually the same at work where men are often directors of companies.  In the society, people hold onto traditional values. Men are supposed to protect women, to rule the society, whereas women cater family needs and educate the society throughout the education of children. However women are becoming more independent, and are claiming more responsibilities in the society. The best illustration of this trend is the president of Liberia: A woman. Things are slowly moving

text: The last presentation which I assisted was enough clear. The presenter opened his presentation by a nice little story about him and one of his clients. Then he started by summarizing the key points of his presentation with as first point an explanation about success of the firm in this moment, the sales figures rose by 10 percent last year and could increase to 15% from 10% over this three next years. Many reasons of this success; our team building and our competitiveness mind. To be clearer he used more graphs as line chart and pie chart. After few questions, that brought to his next point, the change of executive team and news responsibilities for the COO and the CFO and creation of many new departments.  However, he could have done differently; we would have understood the key point of this part if the presentation had included a flow chart. To conclude if his opening was compelling and if I liked him to given more facts and figures, his conclusion, with his joke which didnt work, had finished with a boring sense.

Currently, the roles of males are  the same than the past but the roles of females over the years have consideradly changed for more leading and responsability although there are between man and woman still difference. In France, the 20th century was an improvement according to the women place in the society. Before the Second World War, the role of women was to stay at home, to take care of childrens and to educate them. But during the war, women invested in factories to replace men. The war as been a rotative for women. In fact, the place according to women has increase. They obtain the right to vote, to right to work without the agreement of their husband... Gradually, they have a most important place in the society and mainly in the world of work where we can see more and more women as CEO or with an important place. However, there is still not equality between men and women. For exemple, the women's wage is often lower than the men's one. And theyis still inequality in politic where the place of women is still not important even if they represent the majority of the population. But we can says that the french society has made huge progress during the last century.

In all Europe, improvements have been made in the subject of gender equally for 50 years. After the voting right, the abortion right was obtained in 1980. That was an important social advance as the victory was won side by side with men and not against them.  However, we have to continue to seek the total equality for example about wages.  By the time,  the gender concerns are very important within specific politic regims which try to lower the women rights level. That's why people, step by step and month after month, getting strapped and even broke. What a mess. Why gender equality is important in France? The gender equality exists for a long time in the French constitution. However, it is still a hot topic in my country. In the current government, there is a Ministry of the women rights. During the 20s, the role of women in France changed dramatically. Two events were mainly responsible: World War II and "May 68". Before World War II, women's role in French society was clearly defined: Their place was in the home, raising a family as their husbands went out to work.  During the war, the woman replaced the man in factories and agriculture fields. The events of "May 68" upset traditional values by the legalization of the pillule and the abortion, what gives to certain women the feeling to be more independent. At present, the debate concerns to the parity between men and women in the political and economic power.

I used to say that Im someone who's pretty good to manage his finances. Obviously, I already have encountered a lack of money. Especially since I moved and get a new job. Living cost progressively increases, housing budget literally exploded, my insurance and my motorcycle's mortgage as well. I had to borrow some money to my mother, which wasn't easy as I consider myself as independent. I also had to change the way I managed my finances, and start from a scratch my new monthly budget. But, thanks to that, I could keep a roof over my head, and food on the table. Now, things are turning around, and I'm saving money.

Just a few years ago, our reading was limited to the printed page. Digital publishing changed all that. People read on tablets, e-readers, laptops, desktop computers, phones and even wearable computers. Instead of carrying around a heavy backpack of textbooks, students can do all of their schoolwork on a tablet. What if you don't understand something in your digital textbook? Just tap on a link on your gadget; a pop-up window immediately provides you with helpful information. Even better, a video might pop up to provide an explanation. As a bonus, electronic communications media allows you to change the font size to make things easier to read.

text: What makes a good leadership is a combination of several qualities such as trustworthy, understandable, patient. The key element to master to possess a good leadership is adaptation. It is important to understand each people state of mind and abilities in order to extract the best of your co-workers. I wonder if there are manners and etiquette to respect nowadays. I think, it might depend on your social class or the one of the persons who organise the dinner party. I belong to a family of industry workers and little farmers and my parents always receive people from the same class. There is no real etiquette to follow, no special attention to have toward a person regarding her job or the money she has. But, there is a natural and mutual respect, because nobody has an easy life and everyone do his best. Words that are used are normal ones and, in these occasions, there is a tendancy to try and avoid slang language and too familiar words. Depending on the presence of grandparents or children, technical language or adults word are less used, I mean internet or sms language and sexual humor. A natural politeness guide our behavior without exageration or hyprocrisy.

I have never thought about office politics in my job or at school, because it is not my practice. I try to do my job the most effectively possible, doing my better in every situation and I believe that thanks to that, I could progress in my career and my life. Maybe I'm too naive because I know certain people try to influence the hierarchy but it's not my way. I never go on parties or things like that where are the decision-makers because I don't like that and I don't feel at my place. Perhaps one day I'll play the game if I see I don't advance as I want. In the meantime I do my work and try to not annoy or disappoint my bosses. I'm confident of myself and I know my manager see how I am important in the company.

To answer this question, I would say in the first place : yes, I do believe that important companies have a big role to play in their public image and in the general behavior of consumers. Such those companies are carrying an important image and they look like examples or even references to thousands of people. For instance, let's take Apple Inc.. Steve Jobs changed the life of millions of people by developing a new vision of computer softwares with Mac. And then people starting to think differently about the Internet and new technologies. They now see it as a place where everything must be simple to use and intuitive. But Apple has also an image to keep : they must avoid such scandals as those with the Foxconn company, which is in charge of iPhone building. To finish, I would say that big companies have an important part to play. They must carry a good image of businness, and not the idea that their belong only to their shareolders. Montpellier is situated, in the South of France a few miles away from the Mediterranean coast. But the tides are not very strong, so the tidal energy is not the right renewable power to choose for this place. Alternatively, the region of Montpellier is crossed by a stong river, called "Le Lez", that might be a great potential for the local population, as well as for the environment. So, a dam could be built accross The Lez. And since Montpellier population is increasing steadily , this investment would not cost a lot, because 450000 inhabitants can easily raise $75000 project costs, as part of their local taxes. In addition, that there would be no fuel costs for this project, and the maintenance cost will be very low. Consequently, Montpellier population would use a renewable energy, for a lower cost than the energy they are using now. If I were the mayor of Montpellier, I would do my best to save my city from pollution, and thus preserve the environment. In my opinion, using hydroelectric energy in Montpellier would be a big step towards building an environmentally friendly society. It seems as simple as that, but politicians don't really practice what preach.%%

Success is often associated with financial and social success. The image that comes to my mind when I think of success is a hard-working and busy businessman, who is very rich and respected by people. I imagine a man who runs behind schedule but who has the time to show his wonderful and elegant woman during fashionable evenings. Businessmen are respected because they embody the power. On the contrary, a spiritual person like Soeur Emmanuelle, who owned nothing, was admirable because she was herself and made us aware of the essential things. I consider that Soeur Emmanuelle succeeded her personal life because she remained faithful to herself and to her deepest aspirations. She found the meaning of her life by believing in God and by helping the most needy. However, you can find some similarities between these two examples. The businessman and the spiritual person both have a goal and do their best to reach it. They both have qualities and skills that lead them to success like intelligence, a great involvement in what they do, an extraordinary strength of character and perseverance. Despite these similarities, I cant help thinking that the businessman is just gifted like thousands of businessmen whereas Soeur Emmanuelle is an exceptional human being.%%

Every day you can observe that companies are more worried about competitiveness than about respect for their employees or environment. Lately, there was a big fire in Bangladesh in which quite a lot of European and American firms were involved. How many workers, mostly women, died in this accident? How many CEO felt directly or indirectly responsible?  Dont you remember, too, the oil spills in Brittany, Alaska and Gulf of Mexico? The only law seems to be competitiveness. Market shares, shareholders dictate their rules and corporate social responsibility seems to be no more than a wishful thinking. Reality being what it is, would it be responsible to give up? There are so many powerful and gigantic international groups dominating not only economy and finance but also news and our minds that the temptation is to get desperate. Are we allowed to give up? Did Muhamad Yunus, Nobel Peace Prize, founder of the Grameen Bank give up?  In one of the poorest countries in the world, he invented new economical and successful concepts. In our own country, France, the social economy won today a true victory :Les Atelires who were supposed to be defeated, two days ago, were given enough money, from private donators, to save their jobs.%% What a symbol to celebrate the day of the women tomorrow!

Green business is important to me. To be truly green, a business should consider using renewable energy, such as solar or wind power, or an office with a green roof. Businesses that sell green products have to conserve natural resources as much as possible by using organic, recycled and biodegradable materials in their products and packaging. I don't go out of my way to buy green products, but I will pay a lot more for certain items. It's not always easy to find truly green products, and I don't want to use gasoline by driving to buy green products. However, I am concerned about the food I eat, so I sacrifice other luxuries in my life to make sure I'm eating healthy food. Of course, you have to take a company at its word that the product is truly green, which is why I buy from companies I know a lot about.

During the latest 30 years of the XXth. century, many famous landmarks were built in Paris: Centre Georges Pompidou, Montparnasse Tower, Louvre Pyramid, and Bastille Opera. Some of them were very controversial. But one thing is certain, they all will left a long lasting footprint in the urban landscape. In my opinion, the Centre Georges Pompidou is the most obvious failure. Its groundbreaking architecture and its garish aggressive colors are unnecessarily provocative and upsetting. In opposite, the Louvre Pyramid is certainly an unquestionable achievement. This magnificent glass and steel structure in the center courtyard stands out against the ancient architecture of the Louvre palace. It has become the main entrance of the place. Each visitor can experience how this museum is modern and living and no more as asleep as it was before Pyramid's construction. To my taste, it's a masterpiece. Unfortunately, nothing that deserves to be mentioned has been built for ten years. The architectural policy is static since the mid 90's The Seine banks have been refurbished and cleared of cars. An important part of them are now a pedestrian area. But that's an improvement, not an ambitious urban policy. Without doubt, Paris needs a challenging architectural vision to be able to compete with other world renowned metropolises.%%

Well, I gave a look at the list of propositions we had from some companies to choose a kind of renewable energy for our city. I choose wind power. The reason why I choosed that particular kind of energy is for several reasons : First, we here live by the sea, and the city is build alongside the shores, and we have wind all days. Second, the impact of the installation on the environment is quite low, so we could think about put turbines in many different places without have the fear of destructing the ecosystem if we do that. But I know, the cost is non-negligible (80000$), but I think if we do this, in less than ten years we should be able to reduce our cost of electricity by twice and save up more money to build new ecological facilities in town. It's a bet, but I'm sure everybody here is willing to do this if this is the better way we find to reduce the footprint of our city and habitants.

It seems to me that successful people have many points in common. First, they live their lives in a positive way. They never stand too long on negative points and always think about what should be the next step to succeed. Whatever happens to them, they are ready to bounce back. In addition to that, they are persistant, passionate and ready to adapt themselves (or their ideas) to the market's needs. Of course, they also have a strong need to be their own boss and need to do things in their own way. However, sometimes, you can meet people who have successful people's traits and habits but are considered by other people as "loosers". Yet, when you talk to them, you are surprised to see they still believe in themselves, feeling that one day their turn will come up and they will be successful. To do so, they will need the only thing you have to get to be successful : the idea ! Without it, they will continue to be a successful person only in their own fantasy world.

I think my town is a masterpiece of the 14th century's architecture. In fact, I live in Avignon, France, where you can found some amazing buildings. First, the Pope's Palace, a huge castle inside the town, where popes used to live in the XIVth century. It is a very touristic  and a wonderful place that you can visit. On the top of the palace, there is a gold statue of virgin Marie, which shows the story of my town and which is particularly beautiful. The front of the monument is impressive and the park just above the palace, on a hill, is as wonderful. It's a relaxing place where I love to go with my friends or family. In Avignon, there are also a lot of small streets, really old and welcoming and some other parks with trees and benches. The library is an old church which has been renovated so we can feel secure and relax there. In fact, you don't need to love ancient architecture to love Avignon, because you can't think this town's architecture is not great, that's why a lot of people come here quite often!

At the beginning, Paris (capitale of France) was a small village. Since the middle age, most French kings wanted to make their mark on this town which has never been destroyed. Paris houses then an impressive number of historical and famous monuments which are worth visiting. What also characterises Paris are rows of buildings along tree-lined avenues.Those five-story buildings are well known for their maintained facades and their balconies on the fifth floor. In the 1830's, Rambuteau conceived this architecture with wide boulevards and large avenues in order to have the main areas cleared and to make public transport easier. Afterwards, the plan was developed by Haussmann. In addition to those typical neighbourhoods of Paris, no building better symbolises the capital of France than the Eiffel Tower. Indeed, you couldn't possibly visit Paris without seeing the Eiffel Tower which was built for the 1889 International Exhibition. The tower which was the highest structure in the world from 1889 until 1930 rises 300 meters. You can see its top from all over Paris.

The buildings of Shanghai are simply breathtaking in their size and diversity. The city has an impressive number of modern skyscrapers  more than a thousand, according to some counts  that are in sharp contrast to several styles of architecture from different time periods. The Bund offers the perfect tour of sophisticated European buildings, many constructed in the mid-1800s to early 1900s in various gorgeous styles, including Renaissance, Baroque and neo-classical. The area is attractively situated along the Huangpu River. Look across the Huangpu for one of the city's most intriguing architectural contrasts: Pudong, with buildings that look like they belong in a science-fiction movie. Here, you'll immediately notice Shanghai's most famous building, the Oriental Pearl Tower. It's definitely not the world's most beautiful building, but it is one of the tallest (at 468 meters) and most unusual. The spire and antennas reach an impressive 118 meters high. Near the Pearl is the Shanghai World Financial Center, which, with 101 floors, is China's tallest building.

In one hand, there are petty crimes, purse snatching and shoplifting, often occurred by young people, those lead to juvenile detention. In the other hand there are violent crimes: hardened criminals, murderer, terrorist which can not be considered the same way and often lead to incarceration. The juvenile detention gives higher chance to young people for rehabilitation than incarceration, provided that young people can get a new or different education in these special centers. The main goal of incarceration is to protect society from violent criminals activities and second to prepare criminals to their rehabilitation. But the chances of success are smaller as violent criminals are not committed to renew their education, consequently hardened criminals might be considered beyond hope. As far as I am concerned, crime could be considered differently in my country: even if the differentiation between petty crimes and violent crimes is clearly made in the court, but unless there is a lack of juvenile detention centers, young people can be incarcerate as well as hardened criminals. The consequences are disaster as recidivism gets higher and juvenile criminals get hardened.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: We will operate the disaster recovery plan mainly in three steps. First of all, we will restore all the data from the backup site. Secondly, well start all the applications and finally we will ask the users to perform all the tests to make sure that the data restored are consistent.  The data restoration is a process that will take twelve hours since there are four servers to be restored and the average restoration time per server is three hours.  The starting of all applications, we'll take at least one hour and a half. This duration also takes into account the tuning of applications components and the technical tests to make sure that all parts are working properly and interacting with each other.  The users tests will include authentication, basic and complex requests execution and then results analysis.  The success of such an exercise will depend on how well we will prepare it and our capacity to quickly handle any problem not foreseen.

I live in Paris. In this city, there is no very high building but a lot of unusual architecture. The most famous is certainly the Eiffel Tower, symbol of Paris in the world. This tower was constructed by Gustave Eiffel for the Universal Exposition in 1889, in order to symbolize the capacity of the french industry. What's funny is that at the beginning, this monument was not loved by the French and Parisians, and it should have dismantle just after the exposition. During its life, the Eiffel tower was painting in several colors, nowadays it is brown. The Eiffel Tower measure 324 meters and is composed by 3 floors. The first two can be visited by the tourists, with a restaurant in the first floor. On the other hand, the third floor is only use for antennas in order to diffuse TV and radio over the town.

Small start-up rise everywhere in the world and succeed on markets that big companies would like to lead.  But the behavior of these small start up is often to be bought and soon or later to be integrated. These acquisitions give social responsibilities to big companies regards to the process of people integration. Although the skill of integrated people is high and lead the start-up to be at the highest level on the market, it's easy to understand that some practical issues need to be solved: twice support services, twice CEO, twice IT manager etc.... As a consequence, this integration leads to a rapid reduction of human resources and gives to the buyer responsibilities of people selection and replacement. But what would be the final image of the company after this process? The buyer may help or at least gives a free pass to people in their way to find an alternative activity inside or outside the company. The buyer should consider past and future of excluded people . One important reason is not to create your future worst competitor . Another important reason is to prevent bad advertising that degrades the commercial image of owned brands. Give respect to people that are no longer necessary in the organization is surely a corporate social responsibility with payback.%%

Green business differ from standard business in that it offers to customers a product with more respect to environmental concerns and it gives a kind of guaranty that you do not support a disturbing activity. I will choose vegatables of a local producer at the expense of an imported product, provided that the local producer is environmentally friendly. Naturally, local businesses are reducing transport and so the carbon footprint unlike imported products. As the local producer is most probably living in the same area as I do, I have just to go out of my way a little bit to buy his product. In additition, we should consider that tons of vegatables are imported from foreign countries, while arable land are neglected or abandoned, and while people are desperately unemployed. Local business can be considered as natural Green business, in that CO2 footprint is reduced and employment is preserved, to the extent that it is respectfull to environmental concerns.

CSR is a divisive issue. Some people denounce the hypocrisy and the cynicism of corporate social responsibility the sole aim of which is to make money. Indeed, numerous surveys have emphasized that consumers tend to support companies which are involved in a social cause. Although those activities dont provide an immediate financial benefit to the companies, we know how much positively they affect their image. It is often said that it is just a marketing device. Of course, its true but should we expect the companies to be philanthropist? In so far as its a win-win situation, why should we blame CSR? Let me give you examples of Kellogg which plans to provide more than half a billion breakfasts by 2016 or of Sparkly Cola which is going to help underprivileged high school students by donating $200,000 in scholarships. Whether its a PR trick or not, the fact is it really helps needy people. Although companies are free to choose their own policy, they have duties toward their employees who must be respected and fairly paid. Besides, companies which pollute the environment have to clean it up.

If you visit the south of France, it will be worth to have a stop at Montpellier. You will be intrigued by the architectural contrast that has been elaborated since the city's establishment, in the 10th century. The historical city center, built in early middle age, is a real labyrinth. You can be lost in the narrow streets which tell you the story of the city, through their beige stone facade houses, that nestle a number of well-maintained private properties, dating back to the 10th Century. You can also find 19th-century Haussmannian style buildings, standing around the main city square "place de la comdie". The most impressive building at the Comedy square is the theater, its facade is exactly similar to the one at Opera Garnier in Paris. Antigone is the modern part of Montpellier. Built in the 1990's, it confirmed the architectural contrast of the city. The most dominant building in that area is "Languedoc Roussillon region's hall", a daring glass Arch, standing on the Lez riverside, it is 50 meter high, the tallest building in the city center. In Montpellier you can have the opportunity to go through three different eras in less than a day!%%
