I think the question of CSR is very simple and it's suprising that it could be a divising issue. On the one hand, companies are taking all non sustainable ressources on earth for obtaining big profits. What more natural they pay back for that, in giving a better life to society? On the other hand, when companies argue they have nothing to do with society's problems because it's it'the government's job, they are not logical with themselves. If we sould follow their rules, governement would be without public interest! The market is only the one to rule everything. No need goverment and all its regulation! I think all these speeches about morality in companies are funny and really naive. Howether, I recognize CSR is pragmatic, like all the anglo-saxon culture: "A well paid worker is a good worker". Good! Good for them! Good for the company! But I think we forget the real meaning of life, the respect of the planet we have to give back to our children. Not a question about companies but a fundamental question about real human rigths.

I'm a firm believer in sustainable living. I believe that instead of relying on fossil fuels, we should make more use of renewable energy, that is wind and water power, the energy of the sun. Provided that you have strong winds,wind power is quite effective. Building a small wind farm will provide our whole village with electricity. It's a cheaper way of generating energy. Why don't we built our houses with traditional energy-saving techniques. The way I look at it, we can do more. If each individual does their part, we can move mountains! In my opinion, we have to be active ourselves

A lot of businesses exist simply to make money. There are others who seriously which to provide a needed service to a community or to the world. Each of these businesses has a corporate responsibility to the public, its shareholders and the world it trades in. Years ago, a company corporate responsibility was dictated by its government. Today, however, corporate responsibility has to take into account the world that we live in on a much wider scale. The public has become much more globally aware, and there are a number of groups that monitor corporations closely. These groups have the conditions of the world in mind. They think about the social issues of the world, such as labor laws and the exploitation of workers. They are also concerned with environmental issues, such as the rainforests disappearing. Large global companies know that people are watching them and that any wrongdoing will not go unnoticed. Personnally, I think companies must have a social conscience, treat employees fairly and try to do the best for their shareholders while trying to be socially responsible. Corporate responsibility has a huge impact on the local community, but also on the world. Bad and good corporate responsibility has effects that reach from the worker in the third world country to the air that we breathe.%%

There are definitely both pros and cons associated with superstores, but I think we have to be realistic about the circumstances of some communities. I understand the argument that superstores create primarily low-wage jobs and provide few if any social or health benefits to those same workers. I also understand that these giant stores could possibly bankrupt smaller stores that are unable to compete with them. I think just about everyone knows this to be true. On the other hand, if a community is already in economic trouble, with few jobs available and people struggling, saying 'no' to a superstore doesn't seem to make any sense at all. Isn't a low-wage job better than no job at all? And, if a community is struggling, wouldn't the lower prices that a superstore can offer help those most in need? I want to reiterate that I understand that superstores are not the answer for every community. Different communities have different circumstances and different needs. All I'm saying is that, under certain circumstances, a superstore might do more good than harm for a struggling community.

I would like to talk about the nicest buildings you can find in France. I personaly like very much the city of Paris. That's amazing how many interresting buildings we can find here. There are not so impressing by  their respective height but rather to their styles. By going to the center of Paris, where are located the biggest and well-known shopping centers of " Printemps" , " les Galeries Lafayette", you will see here what we call "haussmanienne architecture" . It's a specific style. I do not know with accuracy when it was created but I guess it was in the 1800's century. Now you may want to see more modern buildings. This time you will go the a famous place called " La Defense ". All biggest french and foreign headquarters of business companies are located on a small place as built on a total suface of 25 square kilometers, not more. They are not so impressive by their height compared to the ones we can find in Schanghai for instance. But they are all ultra-modern and regrouped arond the " Arche de la Defense" . This realization has been built by the old French president Franois Mitterand before ending his political carreer.%%

Companies have responsabilities. They offer products to a number of demands, but the demand expects to receive inexchange of its money, a good product or service. That is when the goverment reacts, when the trade ain't fair.  I have example, like mc donalds, which killed a young boy with one of its burgers. They had to pay the price of his death. But again, i have questions because if mc donalds burgers could kill, why are mc donalds restaurents still open. It's not like it happened once or twice. And shareholders must pay to, for giving money to allow those poisoned burgers to be prepar and sell. But how can you juge the juge? And the way meats are provided oh my lord, you give mc donalds to a starving asian or afrikan they would die. Same for coca cola, but what can we do. CSR would be a great idea if the criminal wasn't the cop.

It's not really easy to pinpoint success since everyone can succeed in some things in his life and fail in some other ones. Most of the successful persons we usually admire have been successful in their public life but they did not always met the same success in their private life. as for example, Mahatma Gandhi or Martin Luther King Jr are considered as two of the best leaders of the XXth century ; they have changed the world by showing how to live in peace all together and by helping people to become better persons. However, most of their biographers consider that in their private life they both were far to be perfect, caring or faithful husbands. Similarly, so many popular actors, singers, politicians or businessmen have failed or never succeeded to be truly happy in their private life. Marilyn Monroe, the most beautiful woman in the world committed suicide and Howard Hughes, one of the main shareholders of TWA, spent the last years of his life alone suffering from mental problems... The line between success and failure is often really thin.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: I'm European, more precisely French. European Union existed before I was born so I've known it my whole life. For example, I know that I can travel to United Kingdom, Spain, Germay and almost each European country freely, i.e. I don't need Visa, Embassy authorization or anything else, I am free to move to each of these country just because I want, like any another place in France. As a consequence, I get used with this idea that in addition of the fact to be French, I am European, and Europe is, to an extent, my home. We have a unique currency with an important number of countries, which is something enjoyable to travel. I mean, I use to go to Spain for my summer holidays, and I know that I don't need a particular ID paper, change currencies before to travel or anything else. Moreover, since this summer, fines received with our cars in Spain and Germany cross the border too, and have to be paid to the Spanish and German governments.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear all, I am writing to you to keep you informed about the Diamond project. I have a very good news to give you, the software "Diamond" is finally installed and ready to use. I would like to thank you all for your commitment. First of all, we have to schedule an agenda for the training on the software as soon as possible. Furthermore, we have to book a special room which everyone could look at the projector and make suggestions. I propose to gather some special group belonging in each department for optimizing the time-training. In addition, we have to collect some information to enter the data in the computer as example. I will organize a brainstorming soon, as all ideas are welcome! Please let me know your availabilities. If you have any questions or concerns regarding this project, please let me know. Best regards.

Located on the equator, Dienga is a small city of about 1200 inhabitants. The city council has decided to invest into renewable energy source for its needs in electricity. This decision had been motivated by three main reasons.  First of all, due to its distance from the power grid, it would be costly in terms of time and money to extend power lines to Dienga. Secondly, because of its geographical position, there is a lot of sun over the city almost all year around. The installation of Solar panels takes only a couple of weeks and requires the services of two specialists whose responsibility is to get the proper panels and install them on all people's roofs. Finally, the whole project that amount up to $120 000 will be financed by the local community whose main activity is sugar cane production. For Dienga's people, Solar power is not only environmentally friendly but also is the most cost-effective source of energy.

There is a big number of big-box stores which have been created since a few decades. For instance, on my island, namely Corsica, there are four big-box stores just for Bastia, a small city on the North of the Island. I think such stores have both positive and negative effects on communities. Concerning the pros, such stores can provide a wide range of products at a price more interesting than those proposed by a little supermarket. Therefore people can afford more things and have more choice. They can buy a lot of things more quickly. Nowadays, the big-box stores also propose the delivery of the products directly at home for free. So, it's really convenient when you are busy. However, at the same time, big-box stores shows cons. First of all, local family-owned businesses often go to bust and go bankrupt because they can't keep up with the competition of the big-box stores. Their prices are too attractive. Secondly, the workers of the big stores get very low wages and they don't benefit from social and health advantages. Nevertheless with the economic crisis I keep thinking that it's always a good thing to have a big-box store around the corner.%% I would like to point out that it allows community to create new jobs and revitalize the economy even though such model of store presents some drawbacks previously mentioned.

As far as I remember, I have always lived in cities in which at least one big-box store was located. It is not really a recent evolution of the commercial industry, but it is true that they likely led to the decrease in the number of mom-and-pop shops in the dowtowns. Obviously, people are now used to purchasing their food and cosmetic in the shops of these big retailers, because these companies have succeeded to cut down on the prices when they negociate a large amount of products, with their suppliers. Furthermore, all you need is at the same place and you do not face any problems to park your car for example. To sum up, you gain time and money. However, there are not only pros when a new big-box store sets up in a commercial area. Generally, the market share of the mom-and-pop shops decrease because the craftmen usually apply less attractive prices than the biggest food retailers. In consequence, the social life in the downtown grows poorer, even if some small shops like backeries or drugstores continue to exist. Moreover, the quality of products in the big-box stores is never as good as the one in the craftmen's shops.%% Indeed, you can sometimes hear about food which was not appropriate to the consumption in the big-box stores, because they pushed the prices so lower that the quality would not follow.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Hello fellow colleague, As you know I have been working on a pretty big project for the past 6 months, and it is finally reaching to an end ! I was hopping you could come to my office during the week so that we could discuss about it and maybe you could quickly read my report to make sure I made an excellent work. Apart from that point I wanted to invite you to my birthday party which will take place in two weeks in my house. Plenty of nice people will be there and I guess you too. Have a good day and see you soon !

I would like to speak about the twins tower. I had visited them a couple of years and I can perfectly remember how they were so big, urge and of course high. In the maintain entrance you could find a special garden inside the skyscraper. It was very lightened place and when you entered inside you could feel the space with a lot of glasses. It was amazing to feel that in buildings. Even if I visited a lot of Paris and Geneva for many times, I don't think I will be able to find that same feeling. I felt something special. Not only the garden inside but this skyscraper was incredible. I tried to take a picture to the top and the bottom of this building but my camera wasn't be able to do it. Too hight!! Anyway I love so much New York, the atmosphere is really special and all this building higher make you feel as an ant. When I saw the tragedy of those towers on TV, I thought that it couldn't be possible! But believe me, imagine how urge is the plane! How urge were they! I keep at home a clock broken on my wall representing the twin towers!%%

Dear Elizabeth, Thanks a lot for these three images and poposition for our campaign. You have made a good job. Since the begening of the brand, we use the image of the sportive watch and we focus more on the man consumer and it work really well! The public consider our watches as casual watches. But today, for this new collection, i want more ! I want to surprise the consmer and i believe we should change our trading policy. I have decided that our new selling key is the smart watch which you must wear with the smoking or the luxury jacket for the reception. And i believe we must focus more on the women who wants some beautiful watches to wear with their dress and jewelry. And for this great changement i want use poesy and beauty. To that, i have chosen the first image an slogan :" Century is a timeless beauty".

In my opinion green business is definitely one new way of doing business. That's a new type of business that impacts us in our daily life. It is both a new tool from marketing point of view and an intelligent way how to preserve all natural ressources. But I personaly consider that people are too much manipulated. When watching TV, we are given so many information regarding new products, new brands that, in the end, we are almost obliged to buy some of those green products. Some people even if they can't afford buying them will sacrifice some other things in their life in order to be a member of I call " the green community". Marketing concept has created new needs to people. On the other hand I need to admit that, yes, natural ressources are geeting scarce. It's a just a fact we can not ignore it at all. We all know that the worlwide population is drastically increasing. The earth will have to do more to feed that population but we all know that our planete is already under capacity. In that sense, the green industry is doing its part by providing some solutions such as recycling old products, reducing any kind of waste.%% One good example is the recycling of old clothes into new materials to create new products.

I worked at Honeywell Technologies for several years. I learned a lot from that company. For instance I'd like to point out the fact that Honeywell is deeply involved in CSR. Along the years Honeywell demonstrated in many cases that it contributes to help poor people, support education programs of young students or also is able to create a specific fundation in charge to help people who have been impacted by severe climatic conditions such as hurricane, tsunami, earthquakes....I realized that Honeywell is a well-known company by its products and its quality of service but also because it is considered as a company costantly engaged in many volonteer works programs. For sure it is an excellent way to developp this company's image and keep in close touch with a diversified community all over the world. To give a clear and significant example of what Honeywell is able to do, its board decided several years ago to offer possibility to children of its employees to join Honeywell's academy. That program dedicated to young students from the age of 16 to 19, allows them to discover new scientific or engineering programs. We can think it is a win-win situation as those students with their excellent technical background will have later possibility to apply to some vacant positions at Honeywell.%% Serving local community is also a way how to better communicate on the company and creating futur talents of Honeywell.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Mr. Smith, I'm writing to update you on our progress. I'm sure you will be glad to know that we have tested the samples and determined that the quality was excellent. Although, we identified an issue with some of the packaging materials, we immediately fixed it. Then, the marketing department sent the samples to sever key partners for further testing. Once we receive the feedback, we will develop several marketing campaigns for your review. We plan to mail the materials to you on July. Alternatively, you could visit our headquarters after July to discuss them. In either case, it's critical that we receive your comments by July 22. I can arrange a meeting to touch base the week of August 8th. Could you let me know whether that works for you ? Best regards, T. Bobbeline

I believe companies have a moral responsibility to give back to society. Let me give you an exemple. Sparkly Cola Soft Drink Co. announced today that it will partner with local schools across the state to provide scholarships for underprivileged high school students to attend college. The Sparkly Cola Scholarship Program will award 20 scholarships in the amount of $10,000 each, starting next year. Scholarships will be determined by local school boards, based on need and academic achievement. The people of New York have made Sparkly Cola one of their favorite soft drinks, and they just felt like it was time for them to give back to communities across the great state of New York," Sparkly CEO Sandy Parks said. "Our donation of $200,000 is a lot of money, but nothing is too good for our kids. And we hope to do even more in the future."

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Ms Demaris, I am writing to inform you about our advancement in the new business game. We have finished the third round and are waiting for the rules concerning the round 4. The team want to add that we really appreciated this business game and looking forward to the next round. However, we have one question we intend to ask you. It is related with the grade on this evaluation. We want to know if we need to give you back a report at the end of the game or the grade we'll be based only on our ranking at the end of the game. Kind regards, Elanzize Abdallah (Group B)

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: European Union is an association of the european countries rules by the Treaty of European Union. Thes countries were, after the second world conflict, tired of all these wars which take place in this part of the world. Decide to unite to prevent conflicts and make a political and economical alliance. At the begening, there was only six countries, and they have mainly an economic goal with the Economic European Comunitie ( treaty of Roma in 1957) but since the treaty of Maastrich the organization was called European Union and have political goals to.  Now there are twenty eight countries. Which have a comon currency (Euro), except few countries as England who decide to conserve the livre. They are a European Parliament with a flag and an hymn and there is free trade between the countries. European Union is also a way to fight in the world economy, togethers, they are stronger.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: I am living in the eurozone and I must say, I consider it ,as a good thing. Living at the german border, we have now the same currency, its easy to compare the prices of the goods. In the 90 s when I went to go shopping in Germany, I had to go to the bank and change money, now we have the same. we can travel easier, we have no more frontiers; More and more the two countries are working together like the police for example. Both states are comparing their livestyles and they are coming closer. Its also easier to study in other European countries. They sign a agreement, and can promote free trade, no more customs. Making deals inside of Europe is very simple and brings also people together.The members of Europe belive that theirs closer economic cooperation will make them stronger face to the rest of the world.

Dear Ms Hamilton, I looked carefully into the three images and slogans and my final choice is the image picturing two golfers with the slogan: Golf and Century.The perfect team. It was not an easy decision but let's me explain it. Century watches is a luxury brand, and what's better than golf to demonstrate your premium image. Indeed,the fact to be associated to a sport like golf could bring to the company potential customers and raise awareness to the public. Concerning the ad campaign we have foreseen to carry out several tv spot, radio spot and also advertisement in magazines which target customer are men with means over 35 years old. We intent to target all the different ads to your target customer in order to be the most efficient possible. If you have any questions or remarks, feel free to ask. I look forward to hearing from you regarding our decision. Kind regards. Elanzize Abdallah

The city of Lyon, formely known as Lugdunum during the ancient rome houses a lot of famous buildings or square to visit. The most famous square is the Place Bellecour which is the largest clear square of Europe with around 62 000 square meters. In the middle of the "Place Bellecour" there is a famous sculpture which is a horse mounted by the king Louis XIV. With regards to buildings, the tallest one is the" Tour du Credit Lyonnais" with rised to 165 meters and has 42 floors. The building is known for his shape of a pencil. In France, this building is the 10th highest skycrapers, the nine other tallest skycrapers or all located in Paris. I cannot finish this article without talking about the Mettalic tower of Fourviere which was built between 1892 and 1894 and has a striking ressemblance with the Eiffel Tower. Nowadays the tower serves only as a television relay tower.

CSR is a difficult question to deal with when you are running a business. For a long time, it was considered as a PR trick: companies gave back money to charities in order to have a good image on their consumers, it was also called "greenwashing" especially for companies who's businesses were involved in environment issues. The best example to me on that point is Total : an oil industry which causes a lot of damage all around the world because of their activity, such as the boat full of oil which broke up among the britain's coast, causing thousands of animals' deaths and a big impact on the local environment. It's now a part of the past: according to me, big companies have no choice now to take their responsabilities: new jobs were created in order to promote their actions and try to improve benefits among this issues.

Dear Elizabeth, After a thourough study we have selected the following ad campaign for Century watches. I'm pretty sure you will love it. First of all many people match Century watches with a certain concept of luxury. That's the reason why we tend to choose the picture representing a man playing to Golf, cause as you know Golf used to be considered as a gentlemen sport. But we would like to enlarge our public target by reaching out also women. In this way we have had an idea that will capture your attention : we wish to have, on the same ad, a professional woman Golf player associated to a man celebrity representing a lot of class, like George Clooney for instance. With the two of them, we could reach out a really large public, from almost all men catagories to an important number of women, doing sports or having a glamour side. Please let me know if this idea has fully caught your attention. Kind regards

In my country petty crimes don't send people in jail. If you are caught by police for shoplifting for instance you will just receive a blame, but if you do it several times you will be presented to a judge who will decide whether or not you have to go in prison. However, there is almost no chance that you go in jail. For medium crimes like purse snatching, mug or any kind of such crimes, you will be presented to a judge and probably condamned but due to the fact that prisons are full, you will certainly have to wear an electronic tag. For big crimes, like rape or murder, there will be no escape for you, you will go in jail. If you are just a suspect and before your trial, you will go in custody till the beginning of your process, because you are considered as dangerous for the society.

In my city as I live in the small village, the only renewable energy that would be beneficial will be the solar power and wind power. We don't have an ocean or a strong river in my area, so it will not be possible to use hydroelectricity or tidal energy, even biomass can't be conceivable. Here, some homes have panels solar on their roofs and I have to say that this installation is really costly.  For promoting them to the future owners, it was said that they will save money if they purchase it. The first assumption of this operation was to obtain a tax reduction and the second was to sell the electricity provided by this concept to the government. In fact, the reality is that this investment is too costly and the return on investment is not profitable, because the government of course, dropped the cost of electricity in return. Concerning the wind power I heard that it is not safe for the health, independently the fact of the nuisance of the loudly sound. I think that everyone should take care of our environment by recyclable each thing possible, and try to save energy each time we can do it, because the future does not appear as happy days nowadays.%%

Concerning the wind power I heard that it is not safe for the health, independently the fact of the nuisance of the loudly sound. I think that everyone should take care of our environment by recyclable each thing possible, and try to save energy each time we can do it, because the future does not appear as happy days nowadays.%%

I am living in Marseille which is one of the oldest city in FRANCE. In Marseille we do not have skycraper, but small old building, most of them have less than 5 flours. The city is a succession of small  villages whirh their own characters The tallest building in MARSEILLE is situated by the see, it is the CMA tower owned by the bigest french maritime company. This building is not built to be used for living, it's a business place. More than 2500 emplyees work at the CMa tower. Since the last 3 years a new district is Under construction and will replace ancient factories and ware houses located around the commercial Harbour. Tall building emerge but nothing to do with Shanghai. Even if the city is cosmopolite and open to various culture it remains a regional metropole Economic turn down and unemployment limit the development of business and commercial centers.

In my country, the maxmum punishment for a criminal is to be sentenced for life empriosonment. All criminals even those who have done petty crimes have to be judged. Because of the increasibng numbers of crimes, Poeple involved have sometimes to wait for months before being judged. Another major issue, is life conditions in jaIL. Actually the number of places available in jail is not sufficient in comparison with the number of criminals supposed to be emprisonned. The result is that prisons are overloaded. Where prinsoners should be 2 per rooms, they have to share their cell with 3 or 4 others guys. this creates por conditons of Hygiene and difficulties for the staff working for security. A report was recently sent to rthe governnement in order to force it to take appropriate measure to ensure that kife conditions in jail are respecting human ritghs the Ministry of justice proposed to replace enprisonnement by other s maesures for petty crimes. Theses measure should emphazise rehabilitation.

In French cities, the buildings are a bit older than in other countries such as China or Duba. Firts, if you go to Paris, you will see a lot of old-fashion buildings downtown. They reflect the previous architectural French styles, such as Baroque or Renaissance. Buildings are not tall, and they are a lot of windows in each appartment. Then, if you go to the museums, you could have an idea about what France looks like in the past. The Louvre is a formal royal palace, and the Muse d'Orsay a former trainstation. Each of them have been refurbished to create a place where famous artists and exhibitions are host. I'd say that the most common line of the Parisians buildings are their windows, and their old-fashion look. Museums or houses are sometimes very similar, and it makes Paris as one of the most beautiful city in the world. A place where modern and old architecture can live together.

Dear Elizabeth, We have selected a ad campaign for Century watches. I'm sure you'll love our choice. Many people think that Century is a classic ans well-know brand, cause it has featured some of the most famous stars in advertising campaigns. While this might be true, it's also true that customers prefer Century because it's simply the best and most reliable watchmaker on the market. That's why we have managed to be one of the top worldwide. Considering Century's trend of working with famous stars, we contemplated the idea of bringing a celebrity in the campaign. However, we decided that the quality of the watch was more important than celebrity endorsement.  For this reason, we decided the ad would feature a man playing golf with a message not directed to stars ans athletes but directed to each and every man that will read and look at our ad. "Golf ans Century, The perfect team". Everybody should understand. I look forward to hearing from you. Best regards...

It tooks me a  lot of time to convince this french client that our services are exactly the ones he needs. After two meeting, we fixed it . His meaning of our quality is very good. As you know the french put a lot in havind good quality. The discussion of the prices was a bit critical, but afterall, he accepted them. A accorded them a 5% bonus. Green businesses do their part to conserve natural resources as much as possible. They may operate their machinery using renewable power  such as solar, wind or hydro  reducing the dependence on fossil fuels. Some environmentally conscious businesses install green roofs to positively affect energy consumption and provide a clean, vibrant break area for employees. Green businesses use organic, recycled, reusable or biodegradable materials as part of their business processes, such as furnishing the office or making copies; or to manufacture products, including food, paper products or packaging. People are increasingly attracted to the concept of sustainability. As a result, many go out of their way to support green business and buy green products. There are down sides to this trend, one of which is that some businesses claim to be green or sell green products even though their practices and materials have no environmentally friendly qualities. In doing so, they sacrifice ethics for profit. In fact, some people have become so disgusted by 'greenwashing' efforts that they are put off by the entire green industry.

When its about talking about renewable energy, my country seems always to be absent. Indeed our politicians think that we have bigger problems to deal with, like economic crisis. In my opinion all, if we make the good choice, renewable energy could be a trigger to our economy. I know that the first solution we have in mind for renewable energy is solar energy. Although it has a lot of advantages, he has one of the worse inconvenience: a high installation costs. So if we want to install solar energy, we shall invest a lot of money. For a country like ours it is a high brake. As far as Im concerned, I think we should choose a renewable energy like biomass. In fact biomass is relatively low cost and it just requires forest residue. Like you know we have a lot of forest. The government should just use his power to collect and reuse all forest residue in order to generate biomass.

I live near Paris, the nicest and the most beautiful town over the world. Beautiful because of the size of buildings and avenues. Tipically, buildings are six or seven-story high and were built in the mid 19th century. We have a curious tower designed by Mister Gustave Eiffel and built from steel in 1887.That became the most important symbol of Paris in the world. This old-fashioned Eiffel Tower is about 320 meters high with antennas. Nobody's living in. We just find a famous restaurant on the top and a lovely place to admire the beautiful townscape and to dream about a fabulous date with a really nice young woman. Moreover, we can see the Tour Montparnasse built from steel and glass. Its a dark glass building for business and housing situated near the main rail station inside of Paris. The tallest buildings are built in the area called La Defense outside of Paris. Most of them are business buildings for all kind of companies. Many modern towers were designed over there for a huge amount of business workers. This area makes you feel like youve been kidnapped by a strange community of people, over worked and over stressed, moving around a large esplanade like an antique meeting.%%

Buildings in France are an amazing blend of modern and traditional architecture. For example in Paris, you can admire the Louvre Palace which was built in the 12th century.The main building and its two wings are made of light yellow stones. They are surrounding a strange glass design reminding of a greenhouse without tomatoes : the Louvre Pyramid. Even if you are modern design friendly, you can't prevent from avoiding walking past it. In the heart of the city, when going for a walk, you can go past the big Hausmann buildings. Even if those rough apartment houses belongs to a luxurious era, they are lacking of refinement. In the same way, the skyscrapers in La Defense area are not very attractive. Considering the crowding of buildings,  the place I prefer in Paris is certainly the Trocadero Esplanade, a large area without any buildings, only some gardens and a few fountains. A place where you can stroll and rest from the crushing presence of the overwhelming buildings.

Hydroelectricity To answer the issue of nonsutainables energies ued in our city. I believe that hydroelectricity is the best way to participate in the world movement to save our planete against polution and to reduce the use of fossil fuel. Hydroelectricity is produced through use of the gravitational force of falling or flowing water. And with that, we can provide "green" electricity to all the houses in the city. To install that, we need a strong river with a dam built across It. I have imediatly think about the river which flow near the village. We have all what need hydroelectricity, why don't use it ? Hydroelectricity no required fuel costs and relatively low building and maintenance costs. With my research, i deduce that it will cost the city $75,000 ( one of the cheaper sustanable energy). To finnish, i believe that hydroelectricity can be a way to give a good image to the city ( an "eco-city"). Iam really concerned about saving the planete and you should be to!

I believe companies have a responsability to society, but like eveybody has. I mean, I cant stand the concept of irresponsibily, and Im deeply convinced that, whoever you are, whatever you do, unless you live alone in a remote island, you have a responsability to society. To my mind, the only difference lies in the spread of the aftermath. Therefore, the companies responsability is higher because they are more powerful, and all what they do impacts in a vaster medius. In one hand, companies cant dismiss their shareholders, to the extent that their money fuels their investments. Without them, they would belly up quickly and many employees would be laid off. Nevertheless, they must not consider only the shareholders interest. Too often, you hear about companies which shut down a factory in a country where workconditions are respectful of human dignity, in order to recreate it in another one, where children underfed will work at no cost, and in unsafe conditions. Thats outrageous. In the same way, it occurs that companies harm the environment deliberately to increase their profits. Thats outrageous as well. Therefore consumers must be vigilant and aware of their own responsability. Its up to everyone  not only to companies  that our world doesnt become a dog eat dog world.%%

Anyway, I think it will be some kind of party at work, then we might invite Karl at the restaurant with us all and his wife. Carrie told me last thime I saw her he doesn't even imagine we could prepare such a thing for him. I can't wait,  We keep in touch,  Bye

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: I choose again to discuss the similarities and differencies between the educational system in my homeland (Morocco) and Japan. If we just compare the infrastructures and the organization established in primary schools in Japan and those in Morocco, we will be shoked. Schoolchildren in Japan studies in electronic e-book and everyone of them have tablet so it can be easy for them to transport their school fournitures everyday. They also have electronic educative games that helps them to stimulate memory and curiosity to learn. In Morocco, each student have to transport on his back daily around 14 kg of books, and once he get to schools, he must search for a place to sit down among the large numbre of students (first challenge) and then listen to the teacher. Also, he has access to very few extracurricular activities which is not the case with his Japanese counterpart. This last, has also time to visit museum, do school trip to visit other countries and cultures. Comparing the incomparable make me sometimes sad, but this is the hard reality.

topic: Talking a friend out of a risky action
text: Hello everybody. As everybody knows, we are here today to celebrate the wedding of Guillaume and Julia. I would like to take a few minutes of your time to make a toast to this wonderful couple. Guillaume and I are long time friends and as some of you knows, we have always considered each other as a brother. I would especially thanks Guillaume and Julia's parents for this wonderful ceremony. I remember a time when we arrived at the airport at the end of our holidays, waiting for check-in when the hostess explained us that our number of flight was the good one but the plane took off the day before. Both of us were flat broke and we started a 2,000 Kms Hitch-hiking to go back to our home. Started a wonderful and surprising 2 days trip on the road. That's why I like him, a character balanced between spontaneity and fun. Always keep a good mood, whatever things are. Guillaume and Julia, I'm glad to be here, even proud, and I hope you the best for the future. To Guillaume and Julia !

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: To start with this writing exercise , I would like to underline that I belong both to the French culture and to the Corsican one. Even though Corsica is a part of the French territory, it should be noted that the culture and the ways how people communicate between each other, are a little bit different from the French culture. In general people use both direct and indirect communication. It depends mainly on the context and the relationship between the persons. For instance, at work we use more the indirect speech to ensure friendly contact and polite relationships whereas at home we use more often the direct communication especially when brothers and sisters are speaking together. No need to soften the requests or to avoid conflict, we can be directly honest without hurting the listener. Concerning the body language and the gestures that are common in my culture, we can mentioned that the thumbs-up is really appreciated to encourage or congratulate someone. However the use or rising the third finger should not be used because it is seen as a really bad insult against someone both in French and in Corsica. The V-sign is also a positive sign but tapping your head is frowned up because it may have two contradictory meaning (positive and negative).%% Finally body language and gestures are more important in Corsica. You should keep a soft eye contact and a kind tone and everything would be perfect!

I have chosen for our next ad campaign" Your glamour; your Century" Should we in the slogan add "forever"? I think it could be very fashion to add something which could come up in the mind of the future customers as they will find our brand as the part of their own life. The picture of beautiful woman will be definitely our best argument. This kind of customers like the beauty of everything even if it is costly. Our market target will be associated with a soft song as jungle which will be disseminated in all radios. I propose to you to change the dress of that girl because it reminds us 'Marilyn Monroe' and we have to move with our time, maybe a brown girl will be more suitable. It could be great if we headlight the picture of the man who is totally attracted by this girl. The idea of Champagne is great to show how the party is lovely going and it demonstrates how people enjoy our products. I don't like the light in the left of the picture but maybe we could put a slogan with a picture of our product which will reveal what we sell.%% Keep it up!.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Mr. X I have just received the complete file regarding the bid that you have done to the city of Bulka.I'm going to review it as quickly as possible. I'll give you by e-mail a complete legal advice next Wednesday, at the latest. I'll try to explain why they rejected your proposal from the perspective of the public procurement law. Meanwhile you must be aware that the legal framework has changed in France for the extra E-C companies since 2004. They have to justify that they comply with the social and environmental European regulation. Once, prices and quality of the tender were the main selection criterias. It's no longer the case. In order to be able to give you a pertinent advice, please send me before Friday your social report and any additional information which could bring to light that your company is involved in a socially responsible approach. Its critical that I receive all these pieces of information by the end of the week. If necessary, I can set up an appointment in our offices the second week of January to discuss the point. Could you let us know whether that's convenient for you?%% I truly appreciate your business and remain at your entire disposal for any question. Best regards

In CANNES, you can not find some big buildings and skycrapers like in New York CIty. One of the famous building of this town is the palace of the festival. It is not a so old one, because it has been built on 1979 but on this place it occurs all the years in may the international festival of film. It is not nice because just built with concrete. It shape seems to be a kind of bunker. But at least, a lot of tourists take pictures of it. In front of the sea, near the famous avenue "la croisette", you can find a lot of old buildings. Since a long time in some of them, they became famous palaces like "the MARTINEZ, the MAJESTIC or the CARLTON". It is special type of buildings very large and where you can find ten-storey. Most of them have been built a the beginning of the 20 th century. So they have a specific architecture.

If I speek about green business, for me it's the sale of products which are environment friendly. It also can be products which are produce in our area or country, not at the other end of the world. To be green I think the industrustry has to think about the packaging which often is too much for me. For example when I do my shopping , I always use a bag which I can be reutilised. I need my car to go to work every day but I try not to take it if not necessary. I also have a little garden where I plant  my own vegetables. I buy always tissurs which are recyclable. In my neighborhood many people have solar panel on their roofs; I taught to myself if I have to change my heating I'll also buy a green one which runs with air power and I'll put some solar panels on my roof too. I mean if a company built a new building, they also have to take care to utilise green material which are safer for our planet. My point is if everyone kate a little more care of what I buys and hopely what he puts in the rubish we can go ahead.%%

A lot of people would like to be successful but if so much people are writing about this topic, it is clearly because no one has found a recipe! It is true that one can observe some traits in a majority of successful people: be self-confident, motivated, creative and innovative... But it is not so simple. According to me, success doesn't only depends on you. The support of your family and your friends to believe in your capacities and to help you to realize your dreams is important. Then, if you are born in a wealthy or a successful family, it could be easier too. In this case, your family brings you the money you need to begin your project or can make you meet the people you need. Another point could be destiny and chance. Sometimes, you have the good idea at the good time or by chance you meet exactly the people you were needed to meet! This last point seems very important because in a lot of interviews of successful people they are talking about 'chance'... so, are you born on a lucky star?

It is difficult to pinpoint what are the typical traits leading to success. I could be more concrete by taking an example of a real success story. For instance, we could talk about my friend Nathalie, who recently opened a caf in our home city. That of course required courage to face the bankers and convince them to let her take a loan in their institution, but also passion to be able to work overtime every day of every week, in order to make it profitable. I think realizing this type of plan also require proactivity, because her project was very well planned. This goes ahead with the fact to be innovative because the initial idea, the concept, had to be brilliant. Moreover, creativity has to be highlighted, because she managed to set up a nice place with very little financial means and a lot of ressourcefulness. Let's hope it will be profitable !

text: Everyone, I am very pleased to inform you that the Human Resources Intranet Site now available for You.The icon for the site is located with the other internal site icons.This is a start -up site that will be added to on a regulart basis.At the moment all HR forms and the organization charts can be assesed on this site. What is a successful person? I can't make a complete and global definition. It depends of to many things.Some people believe that success and money are linked, while others consider that a successful person is a person who can help the others, everyone have his definition of success. But i am going to give you few aspects which are to me, the most important to be a successful person. In my mind, a successful person have inevitably to be motivated and passionated, because it is really hard to succeed in general and it necessite sacrifice. In the way of success you have to fail few times and learn obout your failure. A person who is not motivated, determinated or passionated will give up rapidly by discouragement. A successful person have to be competitive to because they are often other person who want the same thing than you and unfortunatly everybody can't succeed, it leads to an hard human competition. To me, a successful person is a person who follow and realize his dreams. And who is happy in his life.

When I left Bordeaux, I was twenty-one and very happy to say good-bye to this town that I hated.  My feelings were more addressed to my family than to the town itself. Now I feel rather like making a declaration of love to my beloved town! What happened? It is not only because it was listed on the UNESCO World Heritage a few years ago. There is a huge change in the way Bordeaux has taken advantage of its architecture. When I was young the town was not only dark but black; the river was brown and sad, you were prevented from seeing it because of the docks. Now, from the Garonne banks, recently pedestrian friendly, you can enjoy the wonderful sight of the Place de la Bourse as well as the long embankment faade a way to enter the 18th century, the glorious age of Bordeaux. The architecture of this century is sober: all monuments are on the same height (3 floors), same style, same soft stone. It is so beautiful to discover the Grand Theatre and the different avenues around that you can forgive, if possible, the origin of such a wealth: slavery and, later, wine.%%

For our city, we have to choose between five kinds of renewable energy: solar power, hydroelectricity, tidal energy, wind power and biomass. Our town is located in the countryside in the east of France and hasn't got any powerful river. That means that we can't use tidal energy because the seaside is far away. The same goes for hydroelectricity because of the lack of useful river, for solar power because of the insufficient sunshine. Wind power and biomass only left. Wind power could be an environmentally friendly and cost-effective solution due to the windy weather. But wind power farms require huge facilities which may injure the beauty of surrounding nature. So, as far as I'm concerned, I would recommend to develop biomass power. Our city is surrounded by large forests and crop farms. They could produce a lot of biomass with a good carbon footprint owing to the fact that it could be made on site. Required investment are relatively low. In addition to that, this way of using renewable energy would provide new openings to our farmer who are suffering from the reform of the common agricultural policy. In these circumstances, biomass would probably be the most relevant solution, both environmentally and economically.%%

Green energy is one of the major issues of this decade, or even of this century. In fact, with the world population growing at such a high pace, energy needs are increasing exponentially. We're now trying to replace nuclear and coil power by wind and solar power to get to a more sustainable world. That's what we call "green business". My opinion about green energy is that it is necessary to start using them massively, in order to preserve the Earth for the future generations of humans and animals that will receive the legacy we will give them. However, hypocritical measures are taken by governments and companies to give the impression that they are acting in that way, while they are doing only the very requiered things. I'm not really going out of my way to buy green products, because they're often too expensive, but I try to act every day in order to reduce waste. For instance I reuse plastic bottles, I buy products in biggest quantities to avoid getting many little packages, and I recycle paper and glass.

Paris is a town steeped in history.The city is well-known for its marvelously preserved historic architecture.Paris is a most tourist-visited city  in the world. In my opinion ,the cause of this success is maintain of balance between modern and historic arcitecture.For example ,the modern very busy district La Defense is relatively separate of historic center. At the same time there is a system of modern and effective public transport.The Parisian museums The Louvre and Orsay are well-known collections ,it houses as ancient as contemporary sculpture and painting.Paris is a very green city ,there are a lot of picturesque gardens and beautiful parcs here.The most famous is Jardin de Tuileries ,near the Louvre museum and Jardin de Luxembourg.I would like to recommend You to visit The Notre-Dame cathedral ,situated on Cit island.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: The differences between universities and "Grandes Ecoles" in France is difficult to understand for strangers. When I was post graduating law in Berlin, I spent much time trying to explain to my schoolmates what it meant. Having studied at the Universities and at a "Grande Ecole", I should have been able to tell clearly about it. But some didn't catch it because it's a little bit strange concept that doesn't exist abroad. Similarly, universities and "Grandes Ecoles" are teaching places which provide means for graduating. The main difference is that everyone is allowed to go to university, regardless of his school curriculum. Conversely to go to a Grande Ecole, you have to pass a very demanding exam which a lot fail. So the best students go to Grandes Ecoles" But unlike university, after having been selected, courses are very easy and there is time left to have fun without studying too hard. This different ways have both their advantages and drawback. The main problem is that it influences the whole professional life. The best jobs are, in fact, reserved for "Grandes Ecoles". It's always surprising to hear a CEO, 60 years old, who is still proud to be a past pupil of a famous "Grande Ecole".%% With the exception of Ivy league and Oxbridge, its a French uniqueness.

I want to tell you about the the famous monnument of Paris : the Eiffel Tower !. The Eiffel Tower was built by the innovative great french architect Gustave Eiffel for "the global exhibit of Paris " in 1889. This tower is only built with steel and is height of 324 metres. This masterful tour have 3 levels using for sightseeing. It's not an apartement building. Nobody,  lives on the Eillel Tower.. You can only go to the restaurant or for admire the view around. The last and highest level is situed at 279 metres and forbitten for public. It' s reserved for scientific and weather exprients. An TV and radio antennas top the spire. This breathtaking tower is place along the river Seine in the center of Paris in an attractive ancien area the Seven arrondissement. When it first constructed this was the most tallest of the world and it lasts over 41 years in the past.  Now Eiffel Tower is not in the running of the most tallest tower but may be the most famous over the world. This monumment is begaming the symbole of the capital of the France. and the most visited in France.

Now Eiffel Tower is not in the running of the most tallest tower but may be the most famous over the world. The architecture of Paris has a really old story : it is a blend between old and modern architecture. Actually, the Paris urban area is the place where you can find the most important number of skyscrapers in Europe. They are located in three different areas : La Dfense, Italie circus and near from the Seine river. La Dfense is located in the western Paris area and gather the most important part of the French skyscrapers, it is so called the business district, the place where the main and most powerful companies have their offices. Italie circus is a residential district in the South of Paris, less than half a mile away from where I live. The third most important place is located along the Seine river, it is a blend between business and residential buildings. On top of that, there is of course the Eiffel tower, the symbol of France in general and Paris in particular.

Nowadays, crime in France becomes a tough problem, such as in many democratic countries in the world. Here, unless the government opens new prisons and recruits new staff, we can't deal with all the people incarcerated. Their solution actually is to let people with sentences lower than 5 years -even recidivist ones- in conditioned liberty. But the problem remains the same : we haven't got enough people to keep an eye on every criminal. To my mind, cutting down on the scurity budget is a really poor idea, even for political reasons. Maybe they should also think about forcing prisoners to build new prisons, with individual cells because... there is this second issue : I assume that most of the prisoners aren't hardened criminal and yet, they are often considered all the same. Then, those who made petty crimes are rubbing with dangerous people who must have bad influence on them. So, another objective should be to split up the upper form the latter.

Although green business is important to me, it would be a lie to say that it is a concept thats is dear to my heart. To be considered green, a business should be using organic and biodegradable materials and renewable energy such as solar, wind or water. These are the cornerstones of sustainability. However, to be truly green, a company should embrace social welfare, (cf. Corporate social responsibility) because humanity itself is a part of the world. It is not enough to treat the planet well, they need to treat people rightly as well. Unfortunately, I am all too aware of the increase of "greenwashing" in our society, which is one of the reason why I wouldn't go out of my way to buy organic products.  We can't take companies at their words anymore. Moreover, I also regret the fact that the products that come from well-know and respectable green businesses are so expensive. As a student, I, more often than not, can't afford them. Nevertheless, when I can, it doesn't bother me to pay a little bit more because there is always a good value for money.

The goal of green business is to encourage "green" practices among businesses. I mean, the first aim is to encourage people to be more environmentally friendly in order to recude and minimize impact of Human Being on nature. Green business is a concept developed to make people more responsible regarding to environment. These last years, within European union, a kind of ECO label has been developed. It aims is to avoid companies that pretend to sell ECO products where as they are not doing as much effort as necessary to consider themselves to be like that or, worst, by doing misleading publicity. To be honest, I don't go out of my way to buy green products but I try to respect as much as possible environment by doing simple tasks: take shower and avoid bath, turn off the tap when I brush my teeth, sort by kind of categories the garbages and so on...

One of the main reasons why Paris is considered one of the most beautiful cities in the world, is the continuity in its urban planning and architecture. I'm going to talk about two of its most strange and extravagant buildings. The Montparnasse Tower is a 210 meters office, one of the tallest skyscrapper in France. It is in the ideal location to discover all of Paris. It is the most central high point in the capital. Facing the Eiffel Tower, it is the best location for taking in the loveliest monuments of Paris. You can reach in it one of the fastest lifts in the world. You arrive at the 56th floor in less than 40 seconds. Situated at the heart of Paris, The Centre Pompidou reveals its unique, timeless and modern architecture. The outside of the building reflects the modernity that is held inside. The building's exterior is very bold: brightly painted pipes and ducts crisscross its transparence facade and an outdoor escalator flanks the building. To put it in a nutshell if you are into arts and architecture, you can't go wrong with Paris. It is one of a kind.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: Incoterms are made to clarify international trade; that is, it defines clearly what the supplier's responsabilities are all along the transport of the goods and when the responsability is tranfered to the buyer, e.g. at departure (EXW), after being loaded on board of a boat (FOB), or at delivery, duty paid (DDP). It also clarify what the supplier is supporting in the name of the buyer, e.g. insurance and transport cost. Into EU, the incoterms are also used but generally simplified to only 2 over the 11 availables. Symply put, the sales are Ex Works (EXW) or Delivery At Place (DAP). Therefore, during the negociation of a sale with a customer, rather than spending time in the terms of the trade, i.e which responsablities are you offering to assume; you can focus on the additionnal services you wish to offer and indeed invoice. Look at it this way, EU offer a large market, with easy terms of trade for a more profitable activity. Mind you, EU is not the world and you should keep on trading out of.

Uno is a card game. The rules are, every player starts with seven cards. The rest of the cards are placed in a pile face down. Next to the pile a space should be designated for a discard pile. The top card should be placed in the discard pile, the first player tries to match the card in the discard pile. You have to match either the number or color, for instance if the discard pile has a red card that is an 5 you have to place either a red card or a card with an 5 on it. If the player has no matches they must draw a card. You must lose all your cards, first player to do it is the winner.

In Mexico manners are different according at geographic zone that are you living or visiting. In south region is a common practice offer a small gift on birthdays, gifts are related to gender. For example if you are visiting a woman, flowers are accepted, for a man wine will be ok,  in some places also small bucket of fruit. But in the north things are totally different, most common practice is a money on a birthday card is accepted. Visiting a friend house is formal to call  at least one day before, is not polite just arrive on the last minute. Other important rules are on a restaurant appointments. Is not polite to arrive late on any date, a lot of people think that arrive 15 or 20 minutes late looks good, but not. Other common practice on a restaurant is order a center dish for all the attendances, if you are thinking to do that just be sure that all people like the food that are you thinking, for example some people hate onions, others can't eat cheese.

text: REPORT: GENERAL EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION AT HANS AND SONS TERMS OF REFERENCE. PROCEDURE: The topics included satisfaction with: 1 Employee basic paclage: including pay scale, number of vacation days, sick days and overtime. 2 Employee advanced package: pension plan, health benefits and shares scheme. FINDINGS: 1 Employee basic package: -The employees were satisfied with pay scales, number of vacation days and sick days. 2 Employee advanced package: -Most employees were satisfied with the pension plan, health benefits and shares scheme. 3 Working environment: -Most employees were satisfied with office equipment. -Most complained that they didn't receive enough feedback. CONCLUSIONS: -Employees needs feeling more important at the company. -Need receive more appreciation of his achievements to feel comfortable at work. RECOMMENDATIONS:  -Remove some levels of management. -Introduce additional informal weekly feedback meetings. -Allow employees to become eligible for the advanced package after 18 months.

I remember a long time ago, I was living with my parents in Mexico, growing up with them. I was at second or third grade in school, I can not recall exactly. The time arrived and my mother had bought the ticket for me to enter and see the magic show. When I was in front of the show I could not believe I was looking at. I was like, Oh my god!. I really was so innocent, I was a young child. The magician was performing tricks of magic like, taking out doves from a hat, taking roses from I do not know where. I had never seen something like that in my life. I was really impressed and asking myself, Is it real? Is that possible?. Then, the magician also was a ventriloquy, you know, the man was speaking so that the voice seemed to come from a living rabbit. That was crazy and completely unbelievable for me at that age. In Peru, a South American country, there is still a gap of inequality in gender roles. Society still expects to shape the behavior of males and females differently. Women are supposed to be more patient, more faithful, more invested in family and relationships. In the other hand, men are expected to be the main financial supporters of their homes. This difference is even bigger in rural areas where is common to observe more traditional values. However, in the cities the generalization of education makes possible for men and women to obtain a degree and gain independence through a job. Many women in Peru raise their kids alone, so they are the main financial source of income for their families. There is also more representation of women running the government of the country, although until now there hasn't been any woman president. There is still work to do for gender equality.

One of the strictest laws in Mexico is the Anti-kidnapping law. Kidnapping is considered as a crime where a person is taken by force, intimidation or deception against their will to an undisclosed place. This law was updated recently in Mexico to prevent the great quantity of kidnappings that are occurring along the country. Due to kidnapping is considered as a serious crime, this law stipulates severe penalties for violation of its provisions and establishes a prison term of 20 to 40 years for anyone convicted, however if the victim is deprived of life, the prison term could be from 40 to 70 years. I think this law is very important because it has helped to decrease kidnapping victims due to criminals that know how severe will be the punishment if they are arrested, however the authorities have been overwhelmed by increases in violent crime related to narco-terrorist bands, while corruption and incompetence have originated a justice system weak which is extremely frustrating for all the people.

Nayarit, a small state, is located at the west coast of Mexico in front of the Pacific Ocean. The Occidental Sierra Madre cross the state through south-north, there on the mountains are living the Coras tribe. This people has ancient rituals to celebrates religious ceremonies, parties and all kind of festivities. The most important celebration take place on Holy Week, during the holy days: People has a religious ceremony afterward start to dance, drink alcohol, chew Peyote, since Thursday to Saturday. On Sunday they go to the Pacific Ocean to baptize children and to make offerings to the sea. The Chaman, a kind of priest and wish, is the ceremonies officer. Like many natives customes, there are a lot of mysticism and mysterious. The religion is a mixed of catolicism and their own believes. However, this people is peaceful and have many poverty. The women working making craftsmanship. The men go down to work in agriculture jobs. This story is not a performing art, but is a piece of culture whose characters are persons.

When you receive and invitation for a dinner party its proper to answer the invitation, no matter how time but it has to be before de date. If you accept to go, you don't have to bring a gift with you, tipically in Mexico all the celebrations o partys are paid by the host without any expectation of receive something. If you are going to a dinner party you have to arrive on time, if you are late, you must call or send a text message to apologize for the inconvenience. Once you are at the dinner party, you have to use silverware in the right way, avoid talking with mouth full and interrupt conversations. In Mexico we used to talk about general situations at the table, except politics and religion, this is seeking to prevent a discussion. Whe you think is time to leave, you must thank to the host for all the attentions.

I have to be thankful because my lifestyle is really quiet and with just a bit amount of stress. But some months ago, the things were not so. I had to work hardly long hours in a big, cold and stressful city and I felt burned out. However, an opportunity comes to move from that city to Cartagena, a smaller and warmful city. I changed my job to work as volunteer as part of a great educational campaign around the world. Actually, I don't get a lot of money but I have enough to get everything I need and even more. I realized that material things are not the most important in our lives and satisfying our spiritual needs is also important. As a result, I turned into a really happy person. My social life has increased because I have many good friends, so I don't feel alone. Also, I am living in the best neighborhood of Cartagena in the downtown, surrounded by beautiful historical places and houses full of flowers. I really enjoy my walking on its romantic streets. Now, I am sure what I want and I feel nothing is missing in my very meaningful life.%%

When I was younger, I remember I was somewhat fanatic in matters related to religion and politics. When I was 25 I changed my religion from Catholic to Christianity and I always tried my brothers and sisters also did, but I think I wasnt doing appropriately because I criticized their religion saying all mistakes that the catholic church has made along the history, but they were offended. I am convinced I work for the best company all around the world. Why can I say it? Well, the office politics are based on deep roots of ethics principles. In fact, it has been our boss who has set the example and rules in behalf of the organization. One of the main statements in order to receive each other feedback is developing sincere love and empathy for the other employees. "Go and introduce yourself, remember when you came here" and "Share the best you have so that the other to get what he does not have " he says.  As a result, that has contributed to build up unbreakable, exceptional relationships among the coworkers. Nevertheless, sometimes difficulties and disagreements arise. Obviously, we cannot bury our head in the sand. Rather, we should apply these useful advices to overcome those issues: First, keep a regular communication with your boss; he has the power to be your biggest advocate. Second, seek the advice of an experienced colleague: There will always be someone who knows more than you. Third, be always well-informed: ignorance regarding some issues could make you a backstabber. Fourth, speak clearly, face-to-face, if you need to express your feelings, but do it with deep respect, don't humiliate or attack anybody.%% Last, love them: they are working with you shoulder to shoulder.

In the US, even in this day and age table manners are considered important. This applies even more when you're eating in a formal situation at someone's house. First of all, if you have any special diet requirements, your hosts will expect to be told about them in advance. Do not spring any last-minute surprises. Take a small gift with you. A bottle of wine, flowers or chocolates are all acceptable. Wait until your hosts being eating, or they say it's okay for you to do so; this shows that you are considerate. Don't make phone calls or send text messages while you are at the table. It's considered extremely bad manners. When it's comes to cutlery, the soup spoon is generally on the right, to the outside of the knife; the fork is on the left; and the dessert spoon is above the dinner plate. However, if you are not sure which of the silverware to use, you can employ a strategy I use myself when in similar situations: Watch what others do, and then do that, too. Good luck, and enjoy your meal.

In Mexico, even in this day and age, manners are considered important. Here are some important suggestions in case you will visit Mexico, some of this advice applies to another cities and remember, manners have to do with showing respect toward others. - If an elderly person gets on the bus, offer your seat, this advice applies to a pregnant women too. - If you travel in a car with a women always open her door and when she's completely inside close it. - If you visit a restaurant, always leave a tip. - Chew with your mouth closed. - If you visit a church, always take off your hat. - If you receive a visit, always offer food or drinks to your guests. - Always watch your language in front of children. - Wait to eat until everyone at the table is seated and ready to eat. - If you are visiting a friend and need to use his bathroom, remember to put the toilet seat down and flush the toilet. - Always make eye contact when you are talking with a person. I hope this manners helps you on your trip.

When I was young my family and I lived in Mxico City, but my mother used to take us to the Morelos State from time to time, in order to enjoy one of the most traditional dances for carnival named Chinelos. This dance was adopted in Mexico after the Spanish conquest as a native tradition into Christian festivals. I really used to enjoy very much the dance and the elaborated costumes. The Chinelos costume consists of a long adorned robe which usually has a rectangular shape, a mask made of mesh and a very expensive large colorful plumed hat. The dance is a set of repetitive steps and the choreography is very simple where the dancers perform two shuffling steps and then with one shoulder or the other, take a small jump to the right or left; but the best is that the dancers are always accompanied by a wonderful brass band. Most Chinelos dancers are young man and their role is passed from parents to children during many generations. Nowadays I continue enjoying the Chinelos dance when the celebration of my neighborhood takes place every year.

I feel like I need a big change for me, I mean, my job, my lifestyle.

Five years ago, my wife and me took a vacation on a cruise and went to the Greek Islands. These trips last about 7 to 15 days according to the Islands you want to know.The ships are enormous with 2000 passengers capacity or even more. The passengers are men and women and childrens of all kind of nationalities. There are many guides of tourists who'll take the tourists to the land tours aboard buses to know the archeological sites and important places as well as the usual places of the island like the stores, the market, the church and the beautiful landscapes. At the end of the day, people comback to the ship at a certain hour to have a supper and enjoy the musical shows with singers and dancers. Then people going to sleep because the ship going on during night to another island, and at the next morning the trip goes on. But the awkward situation ocurred at the fourth day when we woke up at 6 a.m. and after the shower, dressing and the breakfast, get down the ship and aboarding our bus, my wife suddenly told me ' I forget the purse with the money and the credit cards on the cabin under the pillow !%% 'My Godness! 'What a problem' At that moment we felt so embarrassed and made a decision to get down the bus and comeback to the ship to look for the purse. Fortunately, we found it. We lost the tour on that day but the awkward situation had a happy end.

It was an awkward situation that happened yesterday. All started the day before yesterday when a colleague realizes that there was a problem with a production line. This problem was serious and one of the options was to stop the production line to avoid wasting millions in raw materials but in the other hand it also implies to lose millions in sales. When my colleague was telling me the information he was as worried as Id never seen him before. When I receive the information, my first thought was stop the production line but then I realized it wasnt my right to take this decision alone. By the end of the day the note had spread out through the whole company but none wanted neither to make the information official nor to take a decision. Finally somebody give the order to stop the production and it was like lighting a match in a gas station. Today all the options have been analyzed and we are just about to take a decision but the situation wasn''t as awkward as the first person who gives the order had thought it was going to be.

The charreada is considered the national sport; the event is divided in 8 competitions for men and 2 for women. Some years ago I was trying to do something different I traveled to the desert and I rode a horse, I absolutely loved it, so a friend of mine invited me to go practicing the escaramuza, it''s really quite difficult because you have to learn to control the horse, and you have to perform choreographed pattern while the horse is galloping and you use a special saddle that put both of your legs in one side of the horse and you are wearing an enormous dress.
