It's difficult to talk about Corporate Social Responsibility because if we consider that nowadays we are living in an era of globalization and multinational corporations; most of them' obviously are focused on generating profits. However, as result of that situation, they are affecting the environment and the social welfare as well. Fortunately, at the present more and more companies are adopting CSR, although some people say that it's just a marketing device used for companies to improve reputation of their brands. Particularly, I think corporations should not be only responsible to their shareholders; its important to have ethical principles and moral to conduct business in the wider community and make good for the society, but there are powerful arguments on both sides of the issue. On the one hand, companies only are responsible to its shareholders, but on the other hand, community is letting them to do business. For that reason they should contribute to the sustainability of the environment and support the local community by taking actions based on ethical values for the benefit of society.

Crime is among the most urgent concerns in Mexico, as a Mexican I'm very worried. Mexico has experienced increasingly high crime rates, especially in major urban centers, and is repeatedly marked by violence. I consider that drug trafficking were the main problem because it is the cause of another crimes as kidnapping, violent robbery, extortion, car thefts etc. The Government has changed some laws in order to reduce the crime, for example the sentence for a kidnapper or a blackmailer is a life sentence. Another thing that the Government did was the installation of thousands of security cameras around the city and a very large expansion of the city police force nowadays Mexico City has one of the highest police officers in the world with one uniformed police officer per every one hundred citizens, with this increase, violent and petty crime rates both falling significantly despite the rise in violent crime in other parts of the country.

There are three building in Mxico taller than 200 meters, two of them are located in Mexico City and the other one in Guadalajara: Torre Mayor (Main Tower), Torre Ejecutiva Pemex (Pemex Executive Tower) and the Riu Hotel . All of the buildings are measured from the main entrance to the top and include the spires, but not the antennas. Personally, I like buildings with character, more than tall buildings of course, this doesnt apply to New York, which I consider one of the most beautiful cities in the world with all the skyscrapers. Therefore, I like  buildings like museums, with their own personality. In Monterrey, my city, there is Marco, the Contemporary Museum of Art, which, despite the impressive collection of art that it houses, is a masterpiece in itself, with the solid structures, patio, and with all the elements that take us to the colonial times. I like also a beautiful hotel located in Mexico City (Camino Real Polanco) because its architecture includes various Mexican elements such as the patios, the tall windows and ceilings, bright colors and very vivid ambiance.

Mexico City is a blend between the history buildings and the contemporary ones. Mexico is a city created for the spanish people who came here in the 15Th century. They wanted to construct here the new Spain and because of that, they built over the arquitecture of the aztecs. Nowadays we can admire, mostly in the center of the city the ruins of the Tenochtitln city (this is the name of the ancient city). I would define green business as the company that, in order to get economic growth, consider essential the social growth of its employees and users of its products or services, all from the sustainable use of the resources used: water, land, energy, etc... The first and last green company goal has a threefold aspect: creating economic value from social values and environmental. The green business, not only has to achieve the satisfaction of their partners or shareholders through customer satisfaction, selling more, but also it has to earn legitimately the satisfaction of every single part involved in the business activities, starting with its workers, following for its products or services and ending with the "satisfaction" of the environment it occupies, natural resources used and waste generated, care for the environment as his own. Finally, a good green company should try their best to communicate their good work and thereby help generate a sustainable consciousness that affects sustainable consumption. No, I don't go out of my way to buy green products, but I try to help the environment using the 3R's: Reduce, recycle and reuse.

The Torre Mayor is a skyscraper in Mexico City. It's the tallest building in our country with a height of 225 metres to the top floor and 55 storeys. Located at Paseo de la Reforma, it was built by Canadian-owned Reichmann International on the former location of the Cine Chapultepec.Construction work began in 1999 and was finished in late 2003. Due to Mexico City's high propensity to earthquakes, the tower incorporates several anti-earthquake measures. Torre Mayor is one of the strongest buildings on Earth in terms of earthquake resistance, being designed to withstand earthquakes measuring 8.5 on the Richter Scale. Until 2010 it was also the tallest building in Latin America, when it was surpassed by the 236 meter high Ocean Two in Panama City.Since then, multiple new buildings in Panama City have exceeded it in height and the Gran Torre Santiago in Santiago (Chile) has topped out at 300 meters. In 2003 Torre Mayor surpassed by less than one meter the 225 meter (738 ft) high towers of the Parque Central Complex in Caracas (Venezuela). The Torre Mayor was developed by Canadian businessman Paul Reichmann, who also maintains part ownership. It is also part-owned by a group of institutional investors.%% The building was designed by the architectural firms of Zeidler Partnership Architects and Executive Architects Adamson Associates Architects, both of Toronto.

GREEN BUSINESS I think green business is mainly about being responsible with the environment. However, the subject could be quite confusing, because many unethical businesses or government agencies or political parties claim to be green just for money or as a mean to get the voters favor. Environmental friendly products are the ones produced with the minimal amount of natural resources and in an efficient way. Some examples of this are: products made of recycled materials, products made using sources of renewable energy or with biodegradable packaging. I dont go out of my way to buy green products, but I do pay a little more for greener products than for conventional ones. I also try to purchase the biggest presentations to avoid in excess packaging and I take my reusable bag to the supermarket. I also prefer to wash the dishes instead of serving in plastic or paper plates, no matter how many people attend a party at my place.

I want to let people know that big-box stores are not as bad as they have been visualizing it. They are opportunity for this town to start walking to the next level. Second, we are going to have more variety of products. Third, these stores are going to increase the employment rate of the town since they are going to generate several new positions to be filled by people of our city. Finally, please let's give us the opportunity to be a better town, one more modern and open to new things; we are not going to regret that attitude. Thank you, Everardo

The kinds of energy that my city will be more benefit the most are tidal energy and wind power energy. Tidal energy is an energy formed with hydropower that converts the energy of ties into electricity. The tidal waves. The cost of implementing this energy in our city is low. The total cost of implementation is $60,000. Another good option to this city is the wind power. This kind of energy is the conversion of wind energy into electrical energy by using the electricity generated by the wind turbines. This system is perfect in this city, as well, because this city has a huge amount of available land to install the turbines, and also has specific areas with strong winds. The cost to install the wind turbines would be around $80,00. This include the cost of construction of the turbine and transmission facilities. A negligible fuel cost and relatively low maintain cost are the features that makes me think that this is a good option to our city population for save money in their electric bills.

I live in Guadalajara, it was founded in the 14th century. Since then to know, we find different types of architecture. Many styles converge to build the occident pearl. From the Barroco and Gothic buildings, to the modern skyscrapers  style. If you could divide the city on circles, you can see how it was constructed as time belts. On the center, the Barroco style represented by our Cathedral, with their curved arches and celebratory spires, constructed when the coloners arrived to the foundation of the city. The Espiatorio, a Gothic Church with their pointed arches surrounded by straight stone lines. There are not too many high buildings in the city. The architects build it on the edge of the city, tall financial buildings and the new ones, 200 meters from the main entrance to the roof, combining office, apartment and commercial stores, like if it was a total city, the owner's or renters don't need to go outside to do what they have to do.

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: In my country people tend to communicate directly to one another than indirectly, another important recommendation is not to stay too close to the person, as this is considered rude because you're invading their personal space, so be aware about that. Gestures as in many others countries are used, but what I recommend, is to do a little research about the place you're going to visit. For example, the place where I live snapping your fingers it's considered very offensive, whereas in another places in the same country is considered normal. Due to diversity of cultures all over the country, many indigenous are living now in big cities, and they can communicate in their dialect and at the same time they probably can talk in Spanish and even English, so most of their cultures communicate with gestures and they  use a wide range of gestures including bad gestures and good gestures, be aware when trying to use gestures near them, as they may think that you're talking about them and they may try to appeal at you. In overall many different verbal and non-verbal communications are allowed in my town, but just be careful when doing gestures to the people.%%

text: Mr. Charles Parks: About the sales plans you aske me for, I attached a spread sheet and the indications. I think it's a safe bet that the cost of traditional energy going to go up - making green business ideas related to saving energy and cutting energy costs also a safe bet. Building a solar panel or wind farm is a great idea, but probably out of most people's league. But that doesn't mean you can't start a green business involving renewable energy. For instance, solar water heating systems can provide significant energy cost savings (providing up to 70% of a family's hot water when properly installed, according to some distributors). In some provinces, such as Ontario, residents can get zero-interest loans or rebates for installing solar water heating systems (PowerHouse Program), making these systems even easier to sell. If this green business idea appeals to you, Natural Resources Canada's Solar Thermal information is an excellent backgrounder. Two green business ideas stem from this experience. One, as a retailer, I would definitely start stocking eco-friendly products. Two, green products would be an excellent theme for an ecommerce website.

Guanajuato, a colonial Mexico''s city. Located in the midle of Mexican Republic, this city is famous by its architecture, history, culture and artistic activities. As you arriving, its first actractives are narrow streets though tunels, and alleys. The city was builded between hills, mountains and ravines. In addition, since 17th century, there were worked some mines. At the downtown, meet the Granaditas'' Alhondiga, a kind of warehouse in the colonial age. It is a big rectangular three floors building of rocks and mortar. At this place had an important Independence battle. Actually, the building operating as the Regional History Museum. La Paz Square is the most ancient of the city. In this place are located the buildings such architecture, the most beautiful like. Point up, the Church''s Nuestra Seora de Guanajuato, of baroque style. The Municipal Goverment Palace, constructed in 19th Century. The Rul Count House, the Legislative Palace and others, all of them builded in 18th century. The architecture is baroque and neoclassic. Other buildings to see are Juarez Theater, University of Guanajuato, Diego Rivera House and its markets. The most important yearly event is the Cervantino International Festival fulfilled in October, participating distinguished artists of opera, theater, music, dance and other arts.%% I like this city and its culture.

Talking about CSR and companies is always controversial. In Mexico there are many companies that use CSR only for publicity. That means, those companies donate money on a big TV shows, make notice on news papers, and at the end they can deduct those donations from taxes, so those are not true social responsibilities. For the companies that I work make an annual event in a local communities to provide food and clothes to the people, in this case there are no news or TV, but I don't think this is well enough. In the other hand, the customer that we have has a complete program of CSR, they are interacting with the community integrating people in to the supply chain process, they are constructing schools, hospitals and providing a stable job. This is a real case of social responsibilities and commitment with community. This was not an easy task, they are working on this program for 7 years and there are still things to do for many years. This is how mining business work in my country.

RENEWABLE ENERGY I believe that the renewable energy more suitable for my city is solar power energy, since the great amount of sun we have practically all-year long. I am a believer of this, but maybe solar power energy is not widely used yet due to the initial investment that it is necessary to do. Despite the initial cost, I am firmly convinced of the great savings that we can have in the long term. Provided that we really want to be more environmentally friendly, there are ways to obtain the money for the project, such as green-credits issued by the government or you can start with half the solar panels. I know some people whom, after installing the solar panels in their houses, have always received an energy bill on the amount of zero, or even in negative numbers! It is like a dream make true: no more bills to be paid -at least regarding energy.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: since my point of view I totally agree with free trades between the countries,but before we make a agreement we must check out some points. 1.-Level of economy between the participants: for example all the participants must have the same economic level otherwise the richest country would take advantages over the poorer countries 2.- Education: both countries need to have the same level in education on their citizens in order to leverage all advantages that offer the trade, for example, if there is a subsidy the applicant must know how to fill up the documents otherwise the request will be denied. 3.- Sectors: traders must be careful with every sector they are negotiating for example if a country is producing grains to lower cost so they need to negotiate free entrance in order to take advantage of it but on the another hand if they are producing to a higher price that the other country they must to avoid the free entrance in order to protect the branch of national production This 3 points are very important to take into account when countries want to celebrate a trade

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: Mexico has a lot of free trade agreements with many countries, almost every one reduce import and export tariffs. The most important one is called NAFTA. It's an agreement between Mxico, USA and Canada signed in 1992 and started in January 1994. In the same time, in Mexico, some people rise in arms because they considered this kind of negotiation will bring to our country a decrease in jobs, flooded with imported goods, destroy the factories and our land will depend from the external nations decisions. After 20 years operating this contract, its farthest than real what they predicted. The economy increase, the opportunities to work rise more than expected, and the risk of national economy bankrupt is lower than before. I'm sure there is a lot of thing what this agreement didn't solve as it supposed to did, like inequality incomes on the people, the disparity between social classes, and the lack of opportunities to many people, especially in towns where they don't have enough schools and financial companies who helps them to improve their possibilities.

The historic architecture of Zacatecas, in Mexico, is marvelous. The city was named cultural heritage of the humanity by the UNESCO, and its well known for its old buildings;  every building has its own history and style. For example: The Cathedral, which is located over the ruins of pre-Hispanic temples, the current structure dates from 1752 and has a faade of sculpted pink stone. The best time to appreciate the face is in the late afternoon when the sun shines directly on it. This church is considered to be one of the best examples of the Mexican Churrigueresque architecture. I guess that this temple is the most photographed in the city. Just in front of the Cathedral is located the Calderon Theater, it was built in the late 1880s. The building has a French style facade and a hall with large mirrors made in Venice. The acoustic and seats distribution are excellent, you can enjoy any kind of performance. If you come someday, I have no doubt that you will fall in love of the city.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: A few days ago, my son and I went at my oldest high school. He was on a math competition, when we arrived I told him how pride I was about my school. He just told me how awful was the building and the local place where it was located, then I decided to compare whole to whole, my old high school versus his new one's. Old: Located at poor section in the city, Almost 10 classrooms grades from A to J, 40 to 50 students per grade, five class hour per day, there is no course cost, the teachers was so informal. Almost every student use the bus as is primary source of transportation. New: Are located in the highest economic section of the city. They have just 3 classrooms, 20 scholars per classroom. We paid a monthly contribution to upgrade and maintain the school. To get the job, the entire teachers have to make an exam and the lowest preparation level was bachelor. Almost every one goes to the school by his or father's car. Even the difference, if the student wants to improve his self , he will reach his goal no matter on it.

I really admire my father. I think he is the most succesful man I know. Even though it is not easy to pinpoint what kind of traits and habits make someone succesful, the excellent fruitage coming from his wise way to live has helped me to realize why he is who I would like to imitate. His childhood in the countryside was not easy: he had to suffer many overwhelmed adversities. But later, when he was 18 he moved to the city and got a job in a big company. He started as a common worker. That help him to get lots of practical, hand-some experience. Then, he gained the respect of his coworkers. Even more, he was worthy to receive the trust from his boss. At last, he became retired after 40 years being the most trust-worthy and beloved employee in the company.  Therefore, I wonder, what helped him to have such success? I have seen the answer all days of my life: The love for his wife and his sons moved him to get up quite early in the morning for long long time to bring something to his children's mouth. It was not the money the most important thing in his life.%% It was happiness. He got it and money too, to enjoy some of the life pleasures.

I am really proud of the historic architecture of Mexico because it relates different historical epochs; however there is a wonderful and harmonious contrast with the modernism and avant-garde of the modern architecture. Nowadays, we can find in Mexico City several pre-Hispanic ruins and colonial buildings as well. Mexico Citys down town is well known as the place of the palaces because it has incomparable monuments and scenic panoramas of the National Palace and the Metropolitan Cathedral which includes a baroque and spectacular neoclassical facade and five separate naves and beautiful side chapels. Metropolitan Cathedral is the highest representation of the Roman Catholic Church in Mexico. There is also a small and enchanting colonial neighbourhood named Coyoacan which is representative of the old Mexico and includes several famous museums. On the other hand, Mexico has several modern and intelligent buildings like the Soumaya Museum, Twin Towers, World Trade Center, The trousers and hundreds of skyscrapers which are a real monument to engineering. In my opinion, the most representative building in Mexico City is the Azteca stadium. It was built in 1968 however nowadays it is a vivid demonstration of Mexican's devotion for the soccer.

text: Bank in Paris robbed by magicians in Las Vegas- A show hold in MGM Grand at Las Vegas was the place to steal a Bank in France. Las night show at the MGM Grand at Las Vegas, Nevada, presented how the Four Knights, magicians, stolen the Capital Bank in Paris, France. With more than five hundred persons in the show, the presenters showed all the people how they transport to Paris and draw the money out the bank. The magicians hypnotized a person from the public and sent him by teletransportation to Paris, he appeared just inside the vault, and he took the money and brought it back to the MGM Grand. But the robbery did not finish there, after the person came back through the teletransportation machine, it started raining money in the whole theater, so the audience could get some euros to take to home. FBI and Interpol are performing an investigation about the case when they found that the Capital Bank of Paris actually was stolen, so they are interviewing every one of the four magicians to solve this case and get the responsible for it. Reporting at Las Vegas, Nevada; Everardo Hernandez Sagarnaga.

I live in Mexico, here the people with a bad behavior are punished! We have 2 kinds of jails, it depends of your age. If you're under 18 years old you can go to the correctional, I've heard that this kind of jail is less agressive than the adult's jail. Here we don''t have the death penalty, (electric chair,hang, lethal injection,...) no matter if the crime has been a serious one! We don''t have a court or a judge who can see the evidence neither. But we have the life impresionment !! In my particular point of view I don't think the prisoners can be reformed !!! The Nautilus house has one of the most unusual architecture I have ever seen. This whimsical design carries forms of aquatic life to architecture. Its forms are inspired on the nautilus shell, creating an original building, atypical and very attractive to our eyes. Completely integrated with its natural environment, this construction has spiral lines that convey the sensation of relaxing habitat. The multi-colored crystal provides a several variety of chromatic tones of the light from the outside. Furthermore, the house hasn''t internal divisions, gaining a harmonious space. It is an expression of free space, in which the main concept is that inside is an organic home. It has been constructed with ferrocement for a double purpose: first, the structure is an earthquake proof house, and second, doesn''t need any kind of additional maintenance. The Nautilus its surround by many high buildings except on the west, from where you can see a beautiful view of the mountains.

Mexico City is the largest city in the world. Despite its large size and its sixteen million slums, the center of Mexico City is actually very clean, and the architecture matches that of European cities. During the 1985 earthquake many of the historical buildings in the city were severely damaged. Unfortunately due to a lack of funds, and possibly a lack of government interest, many structures haven't been repaired, therefore many buildings appear to be condemned. Despite all this, every skyscraper in Mexico City is a monument to engineering. The tallest buildings in Mexico City are: with 738 ft. the Major Tower and with 801 ft. the Reforma Tower. Another taller buildings are: - Pemex Executive Tower with 693 ft. - Riu Hotel in Guadalajara with 705 ft. - Altus Tower with 640 ft. - Latinoamerican Tower with 597 ft. (this is one of the oldest buildings in Mexico City was constructed in 1956).

Mexico City is one of the most populous cities in the world. Is modern and cosmopolitan, multicultural and packed with historic sites. Mexico city is an exciting blend of Aztec, Spanish colonial and modern art and architecture. It''s also a huge, sprawling city. The Plaza de la Constitucin or Zocalo is the city''s main square. The Presidential Palace, with its famous Diego Rivera murals, is a beatiful place to visit. The surrounding Centro Historico (Historic Center) is a virtual museum of colonial architecture and was built on ruins of the Aztec capital, Tenochtitlan. The Templo Mayor, one block from the Zocalo, is the most important relic of that period. The Torre Latinoamericana is located near to the Zocalo and is one of the tallest buildings in the zone. It is an amazing building built more than fifty years ago. It has an unique observation roof that takes advantage of an equally privileged location in the heart of downtown Mexico City. We can forget the Palacio de Bellas Artes (Palace of fine arts) that is the most important cultural center in Mexico City. It is located on the west side of the Historic Center of Mexico City.

In my city home the tallest building has only 5 or 6 floors, so Id rather talk about another building. Let's talk about the tallest building in my country: La Torre Mayor. It is 230,4m tall, it has 55 floors and 13 levels to park 4 of them underground. Its construction started in 1999 and ended in 2003. This building is equipped with 98 seismic dampers and 252 piles made of steel and concret buried 60m deep, it can hold out winds of 257 Km per hour, its glasses are 2.5 cm. Thick, so it is considered one of the safest buildings in the world and the safest in Latin America. This tower is considered along with the U.S. Bank Tower, one of the world's toughest skyscrapers, and also has the most global seismic tolerance, having a maximum tolerance of 9.5 on the Richter scale, He has endured two earthquakes, one in 2003 of 7.6 degrees on the Richter scale, and in April 2007 of 6.3 degrees on the Richter scale. I'm very proud of this building, and pretty sure that you going to feel safe visiting it.

Dear Alexia, We've selected an ad campaign for Century watches. I'm sure you'll love our choice of campaign. Many people think that Century is a classic and well-known brand, because it featured some of the most famous stars in advertising campaigns. While this might be true, it's also true costumers prefer Century because it's simply the best and most reliable watchmaker on the market. That's the reason why Century has managed to be one of the top watchmakers worldwide. Considering Century's trend of working with famous stars, we contemplated the idea of bringing a celebrity into the campaign. We decided, however, that the quality of the watch was more important than celebrity endorsement. For this reason, we decided that the ad would feature a man playing golf, simple and crystal-clear, but with slogan that delivers an efficient message; a message not directed to stars and athletes, but directed to each and every man that will read an look at our ad. "Golf and Century. The perfect team. "It's a simple message that everyone can understand. I look forward to hearing from you regarding our decision. Best regards...

Technological advances are increasing continuously, so its very complicated to know how will be our life in 25 years, however taking in consideration current technology that we already have. Undoubtedly it will be integrated in most of the devices that we use every day and new discoveries and functionalities will be implemented in our homes. Probably places with major impact could be the bathroom and the kitchen because we have there many useful devices with the most potential for futuristic changes. For example, talking about the bathroom I can imagine how future toilets would be able to test urine for sugar levels, take your blood pressure or weigh and upload that information in the cloud to be sent to the doctor. Our fridge will also be able to scan our body and tell us what's inside and make a recommendation about what we should to eat or just to create a grocery list and send it to the supermarket automatically. Besides, with future advances in computing and nanotechnology we will have the opportunity of seeing new developments in medicine and science.

The skyscrapers of Mexico Mexico is a country where pre-Hispanic ruins exist in harmony with colonial buildings,  We have breathtaking  pyramids, temples, and sculptures.  If you visit Chichen-itza in Quintana Roo, you will love this magical place. With the arrival of the Spanish were introduced architectural theories of classical order and Arabic formalities, to build the first churches and monasteries  The zocalo plaza in Mexico City is 57,600 metres' (240 m  240 m), making it one of the largest city squares in the world. We also have the cathedral, is one of the oldest and largest Roman Catholic cathedral in the Americas, And its called, The Metropolitan Cathedral of the Assumption of Mary  Mexico City had its first 40-story skyscraper almost half a century ago when The Torre Lationoamericana, the tallest building in Latin America at the time, was built. I really admire Fusajiro Yamauchi (NINTENDOs father), for more tan 50 years his only product was a board game called Hanafuda. Afterward in 1963, his grandson attempt to make some money investing in a taxy fleet, following that he tried to managing a hotel chain, subsequently he bought a restaurant and finally, he tried to own a TV channe. he failed in all those business, but he never give up, he is very persistente and an amazingly resilient person. He got a lot of debts, but in 1970 they focussed in a new product: the console video games. In 1975 they started to produce the first video games that turned the Nintendo Company history. In 1983 was born the very first 8 bits console Nintendo Entertainment System (NES), and with it Mario Bros y Donkey Kong. I think that Fusajiro Yamauchi and his grandson knew that every failure comes with a teaching.

Crime in Mexico is a common situation, this is a consequence of drug dealer battle for territory. North area of the country has been affected by crime. Is common to read on the news about murder, kidnapping, extortion. Those major crimes are punished by jail for at least 18 years, in Mexico is prohibited capital punish like letal injection, but in my opinion in this cases will apply. Other minor crimes are not punish by the law, for example minor thief with not violence on people under 18 years, teenagers just receive an advice and their parents need to pick up them at the court. But in case that in the thief is involve weapons, murder, or violence, they will go to preventive jail, and at the age of 18 receive a sentence in some cases for the crime. Those crimes are related to the common people, but all crimes that companies and government are doing, like taxes evasion, they move millions of pesos without punishment.

Respect of the corporate social responsibility, a think it is a very important issue, that contribute to maintain a balance in some negative and positive situations that is happening in our society, this CSR, it has several aspect to analyze for example, there are some rich persons or institutions, who have foundation that contribute to fight against illness like Cancer, AIDS, and some other conditions, these foundations pay the expensive medicines to the poor people that don't have resources to pay the medicines. Chiapas governor in Mexico, Mr. Manuel Velasco said last week that is very important to work with the society and the federal government in order to establish a new educational reform to ensure that it archives the established objectives according with the president Enrique Pea Nieto. Currently Chiapas is placed as one of Mexican states that has the biggest challenge to improve the academic quality of its schools and reduce the dropout rate. Velasco said that during his administration has launched many programs to provide incentives to stay at school, as well as to improve educational programs, including support for feeding. Additionally Mr. Velasco stressed that with the new educational reform, tutors and teachers will be able to determine priorities and decide how to use economical resources provided by the federal government, resulting in major changes in the ways school system go about making policy, generating revenues, spending founds, training teachers and helping to improve the quality of education in Chiapas.

In Mexico, it doesnt matter if you are minor or an adult. Whether you commit a petty or a serious crime, you''ll always go to jail. The place you''re sent depends of how old you are. If youre under 18 years old, you''ll be sent to juvenile detention. If youre an adult youll definitely sent to prison. Also, if you commit a petty crime, youll have to pay a fine or spend a few days in prison. But if you commit a serious crime like drunk driving youll have to pay a fine of $1,200.00 or spend 36 hours under arrest. Whether is a petty crime or a serious crime judges will not erase your personal crime record. It will always be there forever. Even so, there are still companies that hire you even if you commit those crimes. They are interested on what you can do well, instead of looking about your past.

topic: Writing an article about NLP techniques
text: MercadoLibre is an e-commerce website with operations in 12 countries in Latin America where millions of users buy and sell products over the Internet . In MercadoLibre products sold small and medium enterprises, producers, manufacturers , importers, entrepreneurs , retailers, wholesalers , private individuals , dealers, etc . To sell in MercadoLibre is necessary to register with the site ( free) and complete a sale with all data details and photos of the product offered . MercadoLibre has two options for publishing : Basic ( free ) : With a higher sales commission Normal ( cost ) allows us to highlight products, sales commission . Buyers should only register on the site , or search for products and services they need to click the "buy" button . Seller and buyer receive data from its counterpart via email in order to contact and refine the transaction. Then both can qualify to tell the rest of the user community which was his experience with his counterpart , the product and the transaction . The grading system allows buyers to meet sellers path within site .

Though, if we want to help to prevent natural disasters, we need to learn to identify whether the green products we buy are created by green businesses or not. Some industries use brainwashing techniques to sell their products and people don't see the truth behind them. I think the impact of this business is good for the next generations to become more friendly in this topic and more responsible for using natural resources and then, to prevent disasters such as pollution, rainforest, oil spill, acid rain and other more. I am not use to buy green products though, but maybe someday I will consider buying the products to help and provide benefits to the world and the ecosystem.

The historic architecture of Bruges, in Belgium is marvelous The city is well known for its Gothic buildings with their pointed arches and celebratory spires built in roughly between the 13 th and 16 centuries some of the most exquisite buildings are centered around Market Square the heart of the coty if you visit Bruges I RECOMENDED you stay in the Grand Hotel cassel bergh an 18 century buildings with a cnteporary wing that blends in beautiful The hotel is conveniently situated in the center of the old city Near casselbergh is the fabulous city hall the oldest city hall in Belgium the Netherlands and Luxembourg There'S ALSO THE CHURCH OF OUR LADY WHICH HOUSES A LOVELY MICHALAGELO SCULTURE and is also the tallest building in the city The spire itself is 122 meters There is the somewhat overwhelming Concergebouw a concert hall Although I"m pleased the city decided to construct something that reflects the current time the building itself is not all that inspiring ir my opinion overall however I have no doubt that will completely enjoy a trip to Bruges and learn much about world history from our unique city.
